4048310:
Westsher

Westsher is an important military garrison in the Plains territory of Molthune.
5699110:
Quent

Quent is the second-largest settlement in the Shackles after Port Peril. It is on the north coast of Motaku Isle, the Shackles' largest island. Sheltered from the storm by the large barrier islands of its picturesque bay, Quent is known as the finest deepwater port in the region. Moreover, Quent is also known among the sailors as the liveliest and most welcoming of the ports.
10362080:
Nesting Swallow

Nesting Swallow is a small village in the central region of the samurai-controlled nation of Songbai.
24622610:
Lady Docur's School for Girls

1 locations:
  • Greenwall House
27750640:
Statue Street

2 locations:
  • Church of the Muted God
  • Tiantown
28096460:
Algidheart

The city of Algidheart is the regional capital of the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch. It lies to the west of Glacier Lake on the Rimeflow River. From the city, the Rimeflow continues westward into the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The city's buildings are built upon bridges across the river, and rise to staggering heights. They are connected by a tangled network of platforms, ladders, and ramps.
33047860:
Rippleden

Rippleden is a small, isolated village located in the Verduran Forest region of Andoran, along a slower patch of the Sellen River. Its inhabitants are known for their friendly, chatty nature, and the town itself boasts cheery-looking houses covered in thatched roofing. The inhabitants of Rippleden are renowned for their distrust of the residents of nearby Whiterush, claiming that Rippleden and its residents are obviously the superior of the two rival villages.
52362800:
Ordu-Aganhei

Ordu-Aganhei (pronounced ORd AGan-hi) the City at the Edge of the World, is the capital city of the nation of Hongal in northern Tian Xia. It is also only one of two major settlements—the other being Muliwan—in the whole country.

While Ordu-Aganhei is technically the capital of Hongal, the ruler of Hongal spends little time there. Instead, the nation's khan also rules the nation's most powerful nomadic tribe, and wanders with them as the greatest horseback riding tribe. Traditionally, the ruler of Ordu-Aganhei is one of the khan of Hongal's brothers.
57037120:
Erran Tower

The Erran Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat top.

The tower is located on the east side of the Isle of Erran, and is responsible for magically giving life to the area.
57930400:
Taggoret

10 locations:
  • Cold Marrow
  • Droskar's Crucible
  • Forest Elder
  • Mount Carissa
  • Mount Langley
  • Mount Onik
  • Mount Soryu
  • New Jelheg
  • Rhuvasi
  • Ulizmila's Cottage
78959610:
Nanzhu

Nanzhu, an agricultural center of southeastern Quain in Tian Xia, is also known for its sprawling and supposedly haunted hilltop cemetery and the monkeys that roam its streets.
91161620:
Peijia

Peijia is the largely empty capital of the Tian Xia nation of Bachuan, built on the northern shore of Naikang Bay. Established by the country's founder Grandfather Pei, the city was designed to hold up to 200,000 inhabitants. However, as of 7211 IC barely 50,000 people had followed Grandfather Pei's commands and relocated there, leaving much of the city uninhabited.

As of 7224 IC, many of the city's buildings are overrun with monsters, magical plants, and squatters despite a recent influx of investment in the city.
93546680:
Sabertooth Vale

Sabertooth Vale is a valley within the Wolfcrags in the far northwest of the Sarkoris region of Frostmire. Prior to the Worldwound's closure in 4718 AR, it achieved some notoriety as the home of the evil and destructive Roaring Sabertooth tribe of Kellid barbarians.
117838620:
Finder's Gulch

Finder's Gulch is a ruined and abandoned village in the Menador Mountains of western Isger that is home to a powerful priestess of Urgathoa named Illcayna Alonnor. She was transformed by her goddess into a monstrosity known as a daughter of Urgathoa and raids the countryside around Finder's Gulch—including neighboring Cheliax and Molthune—for victims to expand her growing army of wights.
124310800:
Lionsguard

The small town of Lionsguard is located on the western edge of the Southern range mountains of southeastern Taldor, south of the Jalrune River.
128120090:
Sulfur Gulch

The Sulfur Gulch is a large module of an ancient spaceship that once stored toxic gases used for various ship functions. As of 4716 AR it was slowly leaking the gases it contains, giving it its name.
138262510:
Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company

5 locations:
  • Gilded Shell
  • Golden Scarab
  • Immaculate Repository
  • Smoking Ruin
  • Zulran's Equines
154161770:
Nightbinder's Wharf

Nightbinder's Wharf is a fortress located where the Usk River flows into Nisroch Bay just to the south of Nisroch. Shadowcallers are dispatched here for missions abroad, where their mastery of shadows makes them invaluable to dignitaries from various countries. Most of those who hire the shadowcallers are Chelaxian, though private companies also gather here, even though such activities are prohibited in their home countries. Discretion is paramount, and thus Nightbinder's Wharf is shrouded in shadow magic; those unfortunate enough to stumble upon it often vanish.
163571100:
Khundurai

Khundurai is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira that is renowned for its luxurious and exquisite carpets that are exported across the whole of Golarion. While most of these carpets only serve as gorgeous floor coverings, if one knows the right merchant, it is possible to purchase one of the fabled flying carpets so well known outside of Qadira.
170215600:
Tuotuo

Tuotuo (pronounced tow-tow) is a city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other deities other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.
175923020:
Iadara

14 locations:
  • Xer
  • Lasinavel
  • Omesta
  • Siavenian
  • City of Thorns
  • Imvrildara
  • Endless Cairn
  • Thorn's End
  • Century Root
  • and 5 others…
195881400:
Bellezeph Villa

Owned by scions of a noble family from Egorian, Bellezeph Villa caters to an elite clientele of tourists who come to admire the Veil of the Spire, try out their mountaineering skills, or engage in amateur spelunking.
200954760:
Myrnorosk

One of the youngest towns in Iobaria, Myrnorosk was founded in 4661 AR by dwarven and halfling settlers around a series of artesian wells on the eastern edges of the Coreth Woods. It is an open secret in Myrnorosk that kodlak and kodlok beings are held in high regard, and its people revere Erastil alongside other lesser deities with a touch of animal to them. Even lycanthropes are accepted so long as they can control their changes, while those who openly hunt lycanthropes will find few friends and allies in Myrnorosk.
211237010:
Ogrekin Hall

5 locations:
  • Broadfoot Marshals Station House
  • Crestwatch
  • Devil's Own
  • District Courthouse
  • Margruel's Masts
212075050:
Chateau Douleurs

Chateau Douleurs is home to the Caliphvaso family and is located in the north of the Ustalavic county of Caliphas.

Located on the eastern shore of Lake Raiteso, Chateau Douleurs boasts a paradise of labyrinthine gardens, statue-guarded pavilions, strange menageries, and luxurious retreats. The palace also contains the accumulated art, treasures, and decadences of the Caliphvaso clan. Yet few ever look upon the estate grounds or are permitted into the palace's interior, as a legion of deadly family guards defend the grounds with lethal efficiency. Such extreme measures to ensure privacy has spawned countless rumors of orgies, murderous hunts and various sordid family secrets.

The halls of the chateau are haunted are haunted by the ghost of Ralagen Caliphvaso.
223295620:
Star Keep

Star Keep is a fortress on the Mendevian banks of the West Sellen River that overlooks the southeastern plains of Sarkoris. As of 4716 AR, prior to the Worldwound's closing, it was staffed by a number of holy paladin orders who worked independently from the national command in Nerosyan. Rumors along the Crusader Road claimed that the soldiers of the keep launched strikes deep into the Worldwound to track demonic troop movements, or made precise incursions to take down particularly troublesome opponents.

The fate of Star Keep since the closure of the Worldwound is unknown.
227207600:
Gutless Griffon

10 locations:
  • Alyssia's
  • Arbor Ward
  • Crystal Creations
  • Ivy Playhouse
  • Kyonin Embassy
  • Ravounel Consulate
  • Sphinx Gate
  • Starspine Manor
  • Sundown Street
  • Thistleguard Station
240355450:
Thorngate

Thorngate is a disabled elf gate near Tanglebriar in Kyonin. It was deactivated to prevent demons from corrupting it upon Treerazer's arrival and has remained inactive since then.
252342550:
Uthong

Uthong (pronounced OO-tong) acts as the de facto military centre of the nation of Tang Mai, despite it officially having no standing army. The city is located in the mountains in the far east of the country. Officially, Tang Mai only has a defensive force, though the push to expand them into a full-blown army is growing in popularity as many fear war looming on the horizon. Tang Mai's military forces based in Uthong afford the city a degree of political influence almost on par with the capital of Surat Prakan, as the five main political factions and their Chaophraya all keep seats within the city. Uthong is ruled by Chaophraya Patcharin, a deceptive illusionist sorcerer who is known for her isolationist stance and heavily favouring bolstering Tang Mai's defensive forces into a full-blown army. This question of military build-up is becoming a divisive issue, with many questioning the need for a military force in Uthong and Tang Mai in general, particularly given recent abuses of power within the defensive force.
254207200:
Kykar

Kykar is a city in the Five Kings Mountains located beneath Mount Gustus in the southeastern Five Kings Range. It is the largest city lacking a seat on the Gathering Council, and its Lord Mayor Hansin Struckiron backs King Borogrim of Highhelm in the hopes that a centralized government extends more influence to cities like his.
263622380:
Graul Homestead

Graul Homestead is a farm in the Kreegwood run by the Graul family of ogrekins. Having blood ties to the Kreeg of Hook Mountain, ogrekin predation rarely extends beyond the forest, though hunters and trappers who venture in often disappear within its boundaries. In 4707 AR, adventurers infiltrated the farm to rescue members of the Order of the Black Arrows that were captured by the ogrekins.
275942240:
Lord-Mayor's Menagerie

The Lord-Mayor's Menagerie is a public park located in the Capital District district of Magnimar. The park's unusual name stems from the collection of bizarre taxidermy and living creatures on display. Canayven Heidmarch and Sheila Heidmarch, a pair of local Pathfinders who run the Heidmarch Manor Lodge, use the park as a place to display their unique menagerie. This strange collection includes an eyeless, and therefore relatively harmless, basilisk, a tame 14-foot-tall ape named "Prince Mandali", and the egg of a tarn linnorm.
280749340:
Drownyard

The Drownyard is a location in the city of Absalom, in the (now mostly destroyed) Precipice Quarter.

Before the terrible earthquake of 4698 AR, the Precipice Quarter (formerly known as Beldrin's Bluff) was known as the Tri-Towers Yard, the premier finishing school for children of the rich and powerful. The unexpected quake destroyed the academy and the students within, but the quake also brought forth a spray from the sewer that contained the remains of an (previously unknown) ancient necropolis over which the school had been built. The lower reaches of the Drownyard access this old necropolis.

Now the Tri-Towers Yard is better known as the Drownyard, a desolate ruin filled with salty brine and haunted by undead. For a decade after the tragedy, the site has been off limits to outsiders, the families of the dead erecting a fence of black iron around the area to keep out morbid intruders and even archaeological groups. The Pathfinder Society was eventually permitted access to the area.
284911160:
Goatshead

Goatshead is the main settlement on the Firegrass Isle in the Shackles. It was founded In 4642 AR by a trio of pirate captains who settled on the island. The Pirate Council laid the blame for all and any pirate misbehavior against Sargavan vessels on the Firegrass island buccaneers. Made the scapegoats of every Sargavan complaint, these three captains named their settlement Goatshead and adopted a flag with a white goat's head on a black field.
285672610:
Roderic's Cove

Situated on the Varisian Gulf at the mouth of the Chavali River, Roderic's Cove is a small town that serves as an alternative destination for merchants too nervous to dock at the pirate-controlled city of Riddleport.
299750200:
Greenstar Market

The Greenstar Market is an open-air market showcasing typical foods from the Lirgen and Yamasa populations, including live fish brought in barrels, and attracting a large local lizardfolk population. Most of the vendors are Yamasan who brought their farming techniques and products, while the buyers are Lirgeni who, by having similar cultural practices to the Absalomians, were able to keep more wealth. There is a cultural divide: the Lirgeni want to preserve these foods as part of their culture, whereas in the past, the same foods were considered appropriate only for the Yamasan who were considered backwaters. These, in turn, are unsure whether to adopt the food they had to subsist on during their impoverished refuge as a cultural symbol or not.
304207570:
Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages

The Founder's Archive and the Museum of Ages are cultural and historical institutions that share a joint campus in the Capital District of the Varisian city of Magnimar. In Varisia, the museum is rivaled only by the Jeggare Museum in Korvosa.
309173130:
Spiritscar

The Spiritscar is an enormous, trench-like rift located in the northern hills of Kaoling in Tian Xia. Over seven miles long and nearly 800 feet deep, Spiritscar was once the location of a monastery dedicated to Lady Nanbyo, the Widow of Suffering. Dating back to the days of Imperial Lung Wa, even before the formation of the rift, the area that would become the Spiritscar had long been associated with misery. When hobgoblin forces first attacked the monastery, the cultists of Lady Nanbyo triggered a massive earthquake that destroyed everyone and created the current rift. The Spiritscar is now haunted by the ghosts of both the cultists and the hobgoblin army that attacked them.
311901910:
Eastern Lighthouse

5 locations:
  • Devil's Elbow Harbor
  • Devil's Elbow Sea Cave
  • Western Lighthouse
  • Witchlight
  • Zincher's Camp
316539430:
Sianovel Agency

The Sianovel Agency is a consultancy and detective agency located in Erdan Sianovel's house in Westgate. It's sandwiched between a bakery and a woodworking shop, with an eye-catching window display. Erdan is already incredibly wealthy, so his clients' ability to pay isn't as important as the cases they bring him; he only takes on the most interesting ones. Lady Seleenae is one of his most regular clients, serving as both his boss and friend and the business also deals in antiques, though it doesn't buy or sell stolen goods. Chirrup Turley is the shop's secretary, managing the business when Erdan is working at the Foreign Coin Exchange.
323290370:
Karpad

Karpad is a small village approximately 100 miles north of Nidal's capital, Pangolais, just south of the region known as the Atteran Ranches. It has been ruled for centuries by the Barons of the House of Boroi.
335119260:
Caldamin

Caldamin is a small, rural town in northernmost Andoran, nestled between the Five Kings Mountains to the west, the Verduran Forest to the east, and the Dragonfly River to the south. This otherwise unremarkable settlement has become somewhat of a tourist attraction of late, due to the presence of two extraordinary creatures who make their home nearby: a red reaver named Auger and a gray render called Shepherd Graygulp. These two foes occasionally battle each other, and their fights are of such ferocity that people come from far afield to witness the clashes.
349965300:
Free Station

Free Station (sometimes spelled "Freestation") is a town built on the confluence of the Little Vanji and Vanji River in the Mwangi Expanse.

To the west of the city lies the Gutblood Labyrinth: a mighty fissure in the Rechiend's Plains, which the locals of the city call Calistria's Grin and which contains Ghol-Gani ruins.
361240240:
Temple of An-Alak

The Temple of An-Alak is a site in desert of eastern Osirion. It is located between the Underdunes and the Salt Hills.
369474130:
Mount Dowama

The great Mount Dowama towers above the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse. Its snowcapped summit is visible through every break in the tree cover and the jungle's natives revere it as a holy place. Foreigners, meanwhile, often see the mountain as a challenge to be climbed and reach the summit. These clashing viewpoints have led to cold diplomatic relations and other interactions between the two groups.

It is located on the south-eastern side of the jungle and access to it is extremely difficult. Legends suggest that the ruins of the Shory Empire city of Ulduvai are hidden somewhere within an isolated vale.
375335760:
Baldachin

Baldachin is a small town at the mouth of the Frozen Road on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.
383992240:
New Towne

New Towne is the most rundown settlement in the Meratt County region of Taldor. It is made up of the former inhabitants of Jambis, who fled the now-ruined settlement and live in squalid conditions, with many residents inhabiting little more than tents. The residents are still technically under the protection of House Telus, but the last baron, Araig Telus, has been absent for at least a decade.

After fleeing Jambis, many of the residents settled in an area that looked promising for farming. The soil was poor, however, and New Towne has struggled to support itself since being founded about a decade prior to 4718 AR.

As of that year, the settlement was led by Tribune Caphridius Vort, who is considered to be a good man but more interested in opera and finery than rulership and far out of his depth. An aiuvarin upstart named Felsha has been advocating on behalf of New Towne to anyone who will listen.
391866070:
Jorgenfist

Jorgenfist is a fortress in the center Varisia's Iron Peaks, near the Muschkal River within the Valley of the Black Tower. Its walls incorporate the Black Tower on its southeastern side.
402078400:
Falcon's Hollow

Falcon's Hollow is a town in Darkmoon Vale, in the nation of Andoran. The settlement sits only a short distance from Darkmoon Wood and is little more than a logging village, comprised almost exclusively of lumberers in the employ of the Lumber Consortium and their families. With few exceptions, the buildings and their inhabitants belong to the Consortium and totally depend on the overpriced goods delivered by the organization.

It is said that in times long past dwarves settled in the surroundings of Falcon's Hollow; rumors persist that forgotten treasures of their fallen civilization yet remain to be found. Yet today it is a place for the desperate with no other place to go, for outcasts and religious zealots seeking to avoid hindrances brought on by their surroundings.
408299270:
White Grotto

White Grotto is the premier bardic college in Absalom where the students learn the art of using magic to enhance all forms of entertainment. The school is located in the upscale Ivy District.

The college is not associated with any guilds, but pays the Ivy District Council and the district's larger businesses and establishments to allow their students to perform without worrying about repercussions. Students wear uniforms that reflect their rank—green for apprentices, blue for journeymen, and black for masters. Graduates can no longer enjoy the school's support, and have to figure out how they will earn a living themselves.
409995380:
Scallion's Wrap

Scallion's Wrap is a village in Nirmathas whose only reputation lies in the way they prepare for Winter Week. Many decades ago, a pack of wolves carried off the village's sheep and destroyed the crops in the weeks leading up to Winter Week. It was then that a witch, using her cauldron and a magic rock, created stews for the entire village. Since then, the preparation of this event has fallen to the witch's descendants, currently the halfling Kupyn Baro.
418311850:
Clear Waters

The city of Clear Waters floats atop Lake Clear Waters in the centre of the Forest of the Sacred Eye and draws visitors from across Xopatl and beyond. Clear Waters is renowned for its beauty and anyone wishing to travel through the city must use one of the city's small boats, most of which are decorated with a special flower that blooms every new moon. Clear Waters draws in the most visitors during the full moon, however, as during the height of the full moon Lake Clear Waters begins to glow with moonlight.
420123620:
Ralthorn Manor

Ralthorn Manor was once the summer home of a minor Cassomiri noble family in the Meratt County region of the Tandak Prefecture. Now, it is little more than ruins. House Ralthorn was one of Taldor's many minor noble houses; they had made their money in shipbuilding in Cassomir and spared little expense in building their lavish summer estate in the more bucolic Meratt County. Around 20 years after the estate's completion, it was struck by one of the region's rare earthquakes, which collapsed most of the manor.

It was over a month before anyone came to investigate, and those who did discovered all of the family had been crushed to death in their beds. Although the family's remains were excavated and removed for proper burial, rumours persist that the family still haunts the site. Hunters who venture near the manor report ghostly child-like figures that play amongst the remains of the orchard, watched by a stern woman who seems to manifest from the remains of crushed stone.
450331640:
Pediment Building

6 locations:
  • Cenotaph
  • Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages
  • Indros cul Vydrarch
  • Osprey Club
  • Shucked Oyster
  • Usher's Hall
454706170:
Longacre

The town of Longacre in central Cheliax stands on the Fields of Chelam near the western edge of the Whisperwood and forms the regional centre of the Archbarony of Longacre within the Archduchy of the Heartlands. Some believe that it is a gathering place for Chelaxian military veterans who have been censured for openly (or privately) criticizing the government or House Thrune.
455430900:
Halfling Coast

The Halfling Coast is part of the southern shoreline of the largest of the Rampore Isles in the Shackles. The cruel rakshasa Bedu Hanji, who rules the Rampore Isles, has maliciously named it "Halfling Coast" because he used to crucify captured sailors from Bag Island ships there.
455752380:
Free Union Headquarters

The Free Union Headquarters is located in The Coins, a beautiful three-story building created by the freed slaves who make up the organization. Milly Tundall, the organization's director, has her office inside, although she is rarely at her desk, preferring to go outside.
468622120:
Clover's Crossing

Clover's Crossing was a thriving village in the north of the Ustalavic province of Canterwall until it was overrun by a large numbers of ghouls who rose up out the local church's graveyard. As of 4711 AR, Clover's Crossing was a ghost town that few willingly agree to enter.
483512300:
Vale of Green Spears

The Vale of Green Spears is a forest of massive bamboo plants the size of redwood trees, planted 1,000 years ago according to legends by a wizard challenged by Tian Shen] to plant 100 million trees. The reasons for the challenge vary by the legend's telling and include a test of the wizard's abilities and an attempt to house displaced birds.

Harvesting the sought-after bamboo risks becoming the prey of the progeny of the late forest dragon Genseirin, who laired here and had long protected the old-growth stalks at the forest's center.
488403570:
Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company

1 locations:
  • Immaculate Repository
497912330:
Kharif

Kharif is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, nestled in the foothills between two different spurs of the Zho Mountains. It is on the northern bank of the River Ladan, which separates the Northern Zho Mountains region from Qadira's Pashman region. The Northern Zho Mountains are the poorest region in Qadira and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor across the Jalrune River. As such, it is the least hungry for war with their northern neighbors.
531087560:
Chimera Cove

Chimera Cove is a small, nominally Andoren fishing village located on Aspo Bay on the northern shores of the Inner Sea. The coast here is rough, battered by waves and wind, and usually cloaked in fog. The village sprang up during the difficult years following Andoran's split with Cheliax and is named Chimera Cove because of the three large islands just offshore: the Lion, the Dragon, and the Goat. Due to its precarious location, the town has failed to expand and thrive, although its residents have no trouble feeding themselves via subsistence farming and fishing.
537907310:
Gilltown

Gilltown is a secluded collection of crude surface buildings in southern Eastgate, a district of Absalom, where azarketi ply their public trades. The surface neighborhood is a facade for an underground and underwater tunnel community leading to the Bay of Kortos. Despite the lack of formal shops, items retrieved by its residents from underwater wreckage can often be bought here, particularly necromantic items.

The azarketi trade on the surface, while underwater they descend to worship their deities and conduct business amongst themselves. The surface is intentionally neglected to keep the existence of the underwater tunnels hidden from humans. Not only pearls, oysters, and marine materials are sold, but the azarketi also recover marine artifacts from past empires, with those from Azlant being the most sought after.
555506640:
Grand Exchange

The Grand Exchange is the largest money exchange in the Andoren capital of Almas. Located in the East Hill neighborhood of the mercantile Portside district, it stands close to Godsgate, and is home to agents and currency exchanges from nearly every nation of the Inner Sea region. These factors deal not only in their own currency, but also in information, alliances, and veiled threats. Many adventurers come to the Grand Exchange looking for sponsorship to explore Golarion's hidden corners.
556881210:
Torch

Torch is an unusual town in central Numeria that sits in a crescent around a bizarre, black hillock of shiny black stone known as Torch Hill. Atop the hill is the town's namesake, a brilliant purple bonfire that occasionally explodes into a devastating miles-high pillar of flame.
561836890:
Throne of the Troll King

The Throne of the Troll King is a huge stone chair atop a high hill near the Kodar Mountains, which is located in the Southmoor region of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Legends claim that by sitting on the throne overnight at midwinter, one could gain dominion over all of Southmoor's trolls until the following midwinter's eve. Consequently, dozens of troll tribes guard the area around the throne to ensure that nobody attempts to prove the legend true.
564424260:
Remesiana

The Chelaxian city of Remesiana stands at the mouth of the Iseld River, where it flows out of the Whisperwood into the Bay of Solva and the Inner Sea. The Hellknights of the Order of the Nail were based at nearby Citadel Draythe before moving to Varisia at the urging of Queen Domina of Korvosa in 4682 AR. Like much of the southern coast of Cheliax, Remesiana is considered a stronghold of the old noble families who survived the Chelaxian Civil War. They now fight desperately to remain in the crown's good graces as they see their hereditary powers ebbing away.
565913580:
Ptirmeios

The Iblydan city of Ptirmeios is the capital city of the city-state of Pol-Ptirmeios on the island of Tos. By Iblydan standards, it is one of the smaller cities. Many kobolds live in Ptirmeios after their ancestors arrived here on a ship a thousand years ago.
567291440:
Pjallarane's Stone

Pjallarane's Stone is a rock formation in Irrisen's province of Thronehold. According to legend, Baba Yaga turned her rebellious daughter Pjallarane to stone here—and the formation does bear a remarkable resemblance to a woman clinging to a tree and begging for mercy. However, most scholars believe Pjallarane departed Golarion with her mother, just like all the other former Queens of Irrisen.

The stone does seem to have some strange arcane power, however, although no one has ever been able to access it. Some believe the stone might hold the key to banishing Irrisen's eternal winter, although there is scant evidence for this.

The local snow goblins view the stone as a holy site, and occasionally bury offerings to Baba Yaga nearby.
568470760:
Khopesh Span

The Khopesh Span is a large stone bridge that crosses the Crook River in southern Osirion. It was built during the height of Osirian power in -2370 AR, and besides the shallows at the city of Ipeq, provides the only other place to safely cross the river. It is still operational to this day and is a part of the overland trade route between Osirion and its southern neighbor, Katapesh.
569996990:
East Rikkan

The small port towns of East Rikkan and Westpool, located north of Westcrown in Cheliax, are known collectively as the Rikkan Ports. These settlements house large numbers of merchants who trade goods up and down the River Adivian, and as of 4709 AR (prior to the abolition of slavery in Cheliax) also housed the numerous slaves required to operate the Stavian Arches and portage stations. Although Westpool is larger, East Rikkan has more docks for fishermen and travelers.
573534160:
Bosorka

The small town of Bosorka is located on the Frozen Road River, in the southeast corner of Irrisen's Feyfrost province. As with many towns along the Frozen Road River its main purpose is to assist those conveying goods and traveling down the river. It is located within sight of the border with the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, and therefore of a land not eternally dominated by the ravages of winter. No non-Jadwiga are allowed to cross the border out of Irrisen and the Jadwiga guards of Bosorka have to be ever vigilant with the border so plainly visible and enticing.
600907190:
The Grand Alchemist's Tower

The Grand Alchemist's Tower is located in one of the towers of the Twilight Gate, and is the residence of the most influential and powerful alchemist in the entire city.
609479570:
Mohgan

Mohgan, also called the City of the Lakes, was a settlement in the kingdom of Gastash, north of its capital. The city was chosen by Zutha to investigate a qlippoth runestone associated with Oaur-Ooung, which he had discovered in the Astral Plane.

Zutha planned to use the powerful stone to create an artifact that would grant infinite necromantic energy, but when his apprentices awakened the runestone's power, a wave of black liquid began to emerge from it. Zutha and his assistants created an anti-magic containment field capable of holding back the flood for some time, and before they could develop a long-term plan to solve the problem, Earthfall destroyed his kingdom. Since then, the city has been forgotten, but little by little, the barriers blocking the water are eroding, and the surrounding region, called the Keening Hills, is teeming with life, with lakes and creatures of all kinds. For their part, the orcs avoid the area completely.
616681300:
Dinosaur Graveyard

It is believed that the dinosaurs of Mediogalti Island are led by instinct to a specific site within the jungle interior when it is time for them to die. This dinosaur graveyard is said to contain countless dinosaur bones, which supposedly animate and attack intruders. Some legends claim that all of the island's unclaimed buried treasure has somehow found its way to the graveyard, where it lies among the bones.
617829560:
Spire of Destiny

The Spire of Destiny is a tall formation of white rock that rises 300 feet above the surface of Lake Ocota in the Mwangi Jungle. Its unusually regular appearance is the subject of a number of legends. The local Zenj tribes believe it may have been built by titans. A more mythical tale claims that it is the spear of Gozreh, which the deity thrust into the earth in order to bring forth the region's many rivers.
635683930:
Aphet East

Aphet East is a small town in the Footprints of Rovagug that sprung up to support the Aphet Mines. It provides goods and services to the miners, although not all residents are supportive of the practices of the mine's owners, the Aspis Consortium.
636820620:
Fort Drelev

Fort Drelev is located on the north-western banks of Lake Hooktongue in the Stolen Lands region of the River Kingdoms and is ruled by Baron Hannis Drelev, originally under a charter from Brevoy.
639125600:
Grand Bazaar

3 locations:
  • District Courthouse
  • Grand Dance Hall of Kortos
  • Saucy Wench
639905240:
Silverlake

4 locations:
  • Castle Baskraal
  • Dismal Caverns
  • Mudroon Homestead
  • Thelsterex's Cave
655830070:
Eagle's Head

Eagle's Head is both an amphitheatre and the settlement that assembles around it every summer while the theatre is in season. Located in the Kazuhn Prefecture of Taldor, Eagle's Head has existed for many millennia, having been built shortly after the god Aroden ascended to full godhood at the beginning of the Age of Enthronement. Ever since then, Eagle's Head has been a centre for the performing arts which attracts viewers and performers from across the Inner Sea region. Practically, every major work of Taldan drama has been performed at Eagle's Head. Every performer from across Taldor dreams of performing here and that their performance captures the imagination of the people and elevates them as a new cultural phenomena, as has happened to countless artists before them.

Every summer, the land for a mile around Eagle's Head becomes a makeshift city of tents, performers, and theatre-goers that is in constant flux as artists and theatre aficionados come and go as the season progresses. This chaotic atmosphere also proves a heaven for those with less-refined interests. Scheming nobles, spies, revolutionaries, and just plain old criminals all use the cover of the Eagle's Head season to conduct their business, its underhanded side an open secret across the nation. The Brotherhood of Silence, one of the Inner Sea's largest thieves' guilds, has a large presence at Eagle's Head. Many members of the Brotherhood of Silence use Eagle's Head as a place to lie low after committing particularly attention-grabbing crimes in nearby Oppara. Eagle's Head propmaster Rouriss Barthel is secretly a member of the Brotherhood and is the closest thing it has to a leader in the theatrical city.

Because of the amount of espionage, intrigue, and general skulduggery that goes on at Eagle's Head, the Lion Blades, Taldor's secret spy organisation, has a strong presence here. They are led by Myro Gravilla, a long-time regular of Eagle's Head, and between them they attempt to keep a secret catalogue of all the season's intrigues. Unbeknownst to most, almost every season sees the Lion Blades foil some major plot to disrupt, poison, invade, or destroy Eagle's Head. The Lions Blades keep this undercover, this way next season's villain will assume the festive settlement to be an easy target.

For the last five years, the elven virtuoso Tirilee Ambrasa has been the talk of Eagle's Head. This is a near-unprecedented hot streak, most performers are only able to claim the spotlight for, at most, a couple of seasons. Despite being a powerful bard, Tirilee's popularity seems to have nothing to do with magic. Her sway over the attendees gives Tirilee incredible access to the Taldan nobility and prestigious guests from other nations meaning the Lion Blades keep a close eye on her. Tirilee maintains an air of mystery by returning to her home nation of Kyonin at the end of every season.
689216990:
Outsea

Outsea is a town in the River Kingdoms containing farmland, swampland, rivers, and canals. Its citizens include both aquatic species as well as air-breathers. Military service is mandated for all water-breathers and a few landlubbers serve in respected voluntary battalions. Locks and gates separate freshwater and salt water, preventing the districts from flowing into each other. Beneath the waterline, coral reefs and seaweed form underwater structures. Fish, octopuses, and other aquatic animals are prevalent. Mages of Outsea are known for their strong tradition of practicing water magic. Unique spells of merfolk and sedacthy origins can be found only in this kingdom.
692043120:
Yllaldo Castle

Yllaldo Castle is a keep built atop a maze of tunnels in southern Xopatl. It is named for the legendary Arcadian hero-god Yllaldo the Manifester, who lived out the last of his years diligently guarding the keep until the end of the Age of Darkness.
711112050:
Grayfrond

Grayfrond is the name given to a deep part of Usk Lake where the water is much colder than normal. This is due to Kialuk, a qallupilluk who escaped from the north and made this place her new home. Kialuk cannot permanently cool the waters and must use magical and alchemical objects, such as liquid ice, to do so. She is searching for a more long-lasting solution so she does not have to find a new place to live.
713726040:
Mount Ganog

Mount Ganog is a twin-peaked mountain in the northern Kortos Mounts, located on the Isle of Kortos within the domain of the city-state of Absalom. At 21,000 feet tall, it is not the tallest peak in the range—a title held by Arazlant Mox. Unlike the rest of the craggy mountains of that range, Ganog is known for its gentle slopes and small mining communities, but that does not mean that there are no songs and tales told about it.
715654110:
Greengold

The port city of Greengold is the second-largest settlement in Kyonin and the first in the elven kingdom to admit non-elves. Situated on Lake Encarthan, Greengold is a popular port of call for traders and diplomats. Greengold generally resembles any other human dockside settlement, except for the trees that grow in unexpected parts of the city and its distinct yellow roofs.
722941000:
Swamp of the Dark Brood

The Swamp of the Dark Brood is a very dangerous place. Boiling pools of acid and dangerous predators are common here. Many iruxi tribes call this place home, and are often warring with each other.
729025170:
The Doomsayers' Stand

The Doomsayers' Stand is a stone dais amidst the maze of tents in the Lower City, once used by musicians to entertain. Starting in 4707 AR, various preachers of strange cults and faiths began using it to attract new followers to their mysterious dogmas, and the resulting clashes between these faiths have forced the Zephyr Guard to post guards around it at all times.
729080990:
Tower Valballus

Tower Valballus is one of only two truly defensible positions on the Bleakwall in the Ustalavic province of Canterwall on the border with the Hold of Belkzen. It is avoided by both the militia of Canterwall and the orcs of Belkzen, as it is known to be the haunt of dangerous creatures.
736486240:
Dongun Hold

The dwarven Sky Citadel of Dongun Hold, located in the Mana Wastes of southeastern Garund, is part of the magic-bereft Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. While the nation as a whole has a primarily human population, Dongun Hold is almost exclusively inhabited by dwarves.

The ancient forge within Dongun Hold is surrounded by gargantuan dwarven statues, seemingly gazing at the forge within. A flaming inverted hammer is engraved into the handle of every item forged in Dongun Hold to show its provenance.
754601560:
Withershin

Withershin is a stretch of prairie in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas, characterized by several artificial and ancient earthen mounds. There are no established histories of the creators of these mounds, though the obvious candidates are either fey creatures or the Kellid tribes that lived in the area before Taldan colonization. The most prominent inhabitants of Withershin are a tribe of druid and witch centaurs called the Withershins, after which the prairie is named. Other traveling centaur tribes go out of their way to visit this tribe to pay tribute.
772432560:
Whiterock

Whiterock is a city located on the bank of the Yolubilis River, 50 miles south of Kintargo. It has long been maintained by the wealthy House Jhaltero, and originated as a stop where rocks mined from Jhaltero quarries were smoothed and catalogued for shipment to Kintargo, but has since grown into a market town for local farmers and ranchers who do not wish to take the long trip to the capital. Its ruler, Canton Jhaltero, is casual and disinterested, viewing himself due an easy life after securing his family's place on the Silver Council.
773995670:
Concurrent Currency

Concurrent Currency is a currency exchange business run by the charismatic Darelli Gammathumalshire and the affable Bagwell Thomkin in The Docks district. The shop has been operating for over 20 years and has acquired not only current coins but also antique ones of great interest to collectors. One of these collectors is Lord Oved Blakros, who seeks to obtain coins from the Aroden catacombs, always willing to pay a good price, though he is unaware that Darelli is hiding the best ones in the back room. Security at the shop is quite lax, and if anyone discovers the fortune inside, a robbery attempt is inevitable.
775978020:
High Bridge

High Bridge is the southernmost of the two large bridges that connect the city of Korvosa with the rest of Varisia. High Bridge crosses the Jeggare River and its western end opens onto Eodred's Square in the Midland district of the city.
788548180:
Hinji

Hinji is a Chelaxian port city in the Archduchy of Longmarch, located on the Inner Sea west of Longmast Cape and east of Macini.
795112920:
Valishava

Valishava is a temple city sat on the banks of the holy Matra River in the Wide Water region of Vudra. The goddess Matravash is commonly worshipped here in large waterside temples, but the city also serves as a centre of commerce halfway along the river's length.

The city is also famous for its high priest Razdara who leads her acolytes on pilgrimages into the Outer Sphere. Her most recent trip saw her return alone, looking deeply haunted and carrying predictions of some dire fate that she claims is soon to befall the people of Wide Water.
812728590:
Cyphergate

The Cyphergate is a cyclopean stone arch, straddling the entrance to Riddleport's harbor to a height of over 350 feet. Because its height is exactly half of the arch's width, it is believed by many that the arch is but one half of a larger ring, the second half of which is buried within the rocks and silt of the harbor bottom. The origins of the Cyphergate are widely thought to be Thassilonian, as the monument is built from unrecognizable stone and is decorated with mysterious runes, similar to other magically preserved carvings throughout the Varisian countryside. These runes are a topic for debate among scholars, both in the City of Cyphers and beyond. Some scholars believe they have no meaning, as they resist even magical detection methods, while others have formed a group called the Order of Cyphers and call themselves Cyphermages, dedicating their lives to uncovering the truth behind the enigmatic symbols.

This paragraph contains spoilers for the following products: Second Darkness Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The truth behind the Cyphergate might have been recently discovered, almost by accident, by a group of drow elves of House Azrinae. Researching a way of looking back in time to observe the events surrounding Earthfall, the elves believe that the gate was used by Runelord Karzoug to peer forward through time, perhaps to predict Earthfall and avoid its destruction.
818455020:
Keening Hills

The Keening Hills are a haunted highlands to the east of the Kodar Mountains in the Hold of Belkzen, located southeast of the treacherous Dirt Sea. Although the Keening Hills appear green and inviting, they are stringently avoided by even the bravest orcs, who say that whatever lies dreaming beneath its surface should be left to its slumber. The few who have ventured there and returned speak of hearing songs that drive one toward suicide or call one to explore its trackless expanse. Fortune hunters looking for buried treasures in its barrow mounds find that their excavations fill with tar and close up with unnatural speed.
830140000:
Grand Coliseum

Nestled in Katapesh's Inner City district, the Grand Coliseum is one of Golarion's largest arenas, capable of comfortably housing 10,000 spectators. The arena is home to many forms of entertainment, from parades to gladiatorial bouts, but by far the most popular is a ball game called ruk.

Many of Katapesh's guilds field, fund, and promote highly competitive ruk teams, and many former players return to the Coliseum to sell their services as trainers and coaches.
838352590:
Thistleguard Station

4 locations:
  • Alyssia's
  • Arbor Ward
  • Flower Street Market
  • Ravounel Consulate
846286020:
Sirathu

Sirathu has long been overlooked by its ruling city of Korvosa, holding the undesirable honor of being both the poorest and farthest removed of the city-state's holdings. It was a relatively late addition to Korvosa's realm, ceded from Kaer Maga in the Treaty of Sirathu in 4663 AR.

The town is constantly reminded of their leaders' disregard for them by the White Prince's Fountain which sits in the town square. While the fountain was being constructed, nobles in Korvosa promised that it would be enchanted to provide a clean and endless supply of water for the town, so they would never be dependent on wells. The fall of the Chelaxian Empire diverted the Korvosans' attention, and the well was sadly never completed.

In 4707 AR a local girl was found unconscious on the edge of the fountain, which was miraculously flowing for the first time. Since then, this young girl, blessed by Pharasma, has been a galvanizing force in a growing unrest among the citizens of Sirathu, prophesying that change was coming and that the town should break free of Korvosan control.
853935870:
Pitax

Pitax (pronounced pih-TAHKS) is a city in the Stolen Lands near Brevoy in the River Kingdoms. It was once a city-state domain of a petty tyrant but fell a few years before 4719 AR after a failed attempt at expansion.
854553570:
Shamara

Shamara is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located north of the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's southern border with the neighbouring region of Southern Zho Mountains.
855002430:
Smoking Ruin

The Smoking Ruin is a unusual sight in the otherwise gilded and wealthy region of Katapesh's Dawn Gate: a smoking ruin of ash and burnt out buildings, once the site of businesses and homes. According to rumor, one night a portal opened up to the Elemental Plane of Fire, and through it a host of angry fire elementals poured. They destroyed the area, killing everyone and everything in the region. Locals have since referred to that night as the "Dawn Gate Apocalypse", and are quick to avoid the area, as the restless souls of the victims of the event haunt the ruin.

Businessmen and residents near the area have pooled together a fund to sponsor a brave group of adventurers to put to rest these poor wailing souls by finding and burying their bones, but thus fair none have been brave enough to take them up on it.
875368290:
Pure Temple of the Maharajah

The Pure Temple of the Maharajah is built over the spring that is the source of the River Sald in the small eastern hills of Jalmeray. The structure is white and gold and is maintained by bound genies and a huge number of slaves and their duty-bound overseers. The temple is dedicated to Khiben-Sald, the ancient ruler who founded Jalmeray. Every four years, thousands of people make their way up the Sald from the city of Niswan to pay respects to his memory. Even though the maharajah is not buried here (and some claim that he is still alive), the people treat the Pure Temple as if it was his final resting place.
885522520:
Gladiators' Guildhall

The Gladiators' Guildhall is a structure of bone and hide where the city's gladiators train with strange weapons and in unique styles, practicing day and night. Battles are held constantly, with fierce stakes and high mortality rates.
905849650:
School of the North Song-Wind

The School of the North Song-Wind, located in the Foreign Quarter, is a branch of the Magaambya. It consists of a grove of trees with fabric draped around the trunks, creating natural walls within the "structure". This structure is characterized by geometric patterns carved into the vegetation, as well as crystals symbolizing Nantambu. The study of the arcane is paramount here, though it also serves as an unofficial embassy for those seeking to learn about Nantambu culture and way of life. Thanks to the area's natural magic, the leshy are drawn in droves, finding great comfort resting among the trees.
910997190:
Tomb of the Living

2 locations:
  • District Courthouse
  • Westgate Bathhouse
920014000:
He Ming

He Ming (pronounced huu ming) is the rowdy capital of Quain, in southern Tian Xia, and home to the nation's temples and government. Its heroes often demolish property while testing themselves and each other in its bustling streets, to such a point that tradesfolk have a well-known saying about the city: "To grow rich, move to He Ming."
925836390:
Jovvox

Jovvox is a largely gnomish town east of the city of Mivon. Originally built around a remote monastery by the enterprising gnome merchant Jov, Jovvox's weaponsmiths specialize in producing large quantities of cheap weapons, fast, thereby undercutting competition. Their raw materials come from a small nearby iron mine and from halfling and human scouts who also make Jovvox home and scavenge battlefields throughout the region for cast-offs that could be recycled. Kellywells, the current leader of the town, is known for crafting exotic, novel weapons.
926673440:
Starfall

5 locations:
  • Kuratown
  • Plain of Ten Thousand Swords
  • Silver Mount
  • Szamrak's Haven
  • Witchlight Vale
955518510:
Brastlewark

Brastlewark is an almost exclusively gnomish city in eastern Cheliax, located on the western edge of the Aspodell Mountains, at the head of the Brastle River. It is believed to be the largest gnome settlement in the Inner Sea region, perhaps even in the entire world.
960259140:
Radya's Hollow

Radya's Hollow is a mining town that was built near mineral deposits in the Hollow Hills, but after a mining accident that killed several people, the mine was closed, and all attempts to reopen it have resulted in further accidents. During the Ironfang Invasion, the town was taken over by hobgoblins who, ignoring the warnings of the locals, reopened the mine, killing several of their own in the process.
965982670:
Canaroden

5 locations:
  • Limehouse Theater
  • Pegasi Bridges
  • Pegasi Bridges
  • Vizio's Tavern
  • Walcourt
970152600:
Orochel House

The Orochel House is a mansion that serves as an inn for visitors to the city of Pangolais. The government of Nidal prefers to keep outsiders concentrated in one place unless they demonstrate devotion to the Midnight Lord. The establishment is exquisitely decorated, the service is excellent, and the prices are affordable. The only problem is the constant surveillance that the Umbral Court maintains over the place.
982027730:
Helsingbyen

Helsingbyen is the largest town in Almhult. It is located on the tip of Cape Almhult, where the Steaming Sea meets the Gulf of Svallmundr, not far to the east of Roshik.
997862700:
Nadzieja Lato

As of 4713 AR, Nadzieja Lato was a small trading town on the Red Fox River in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood.

As of 4719 AR, Nadzieja Lato was within the borders of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.
1001720990:
Jula

Jula is a coastal village in the southern Sodden Lands, populated by hardy folk who defy the Eye of Abendego and refuse to leave the Sodden Lands for more temperate climes.
1010385780:
Lockridge

Lockridge is one of the three large towns in the River Kingdom of Lambreth. It was founded by Angelidis family in 4394 AR. Lockridge overlooks the rapids formed where the Sellen River emerges from deep canyons cut through the Juviler Hills. The rapids were a serious obstacle for merchants travelling this part of the river, requiring skilled pilots and small crafts. The Angelidis family constructed a number of canal locks that bypassed the cataract and demolished the shanty town of Troxell, replacing it with stone wharves and warehouses along the canals. While the family carefully respects the freedom of the river itself, many merchants pay to take advantage of the locks, especially those hauling quarried Juviler granite and polished agate.

Morissa Angelidis is the elected mayor of Lockridge, travelling to Maashinelle only upon matters of importance.
1015444340:
Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall

3 locations:
  • Damaq Palace
  • Palace Ormuz
  • Uiry Manor Museum
1021198790:
Ker Sharic

Ker Sharic is a secluded settlement located on the shores of the Lake of Mists and Veils in Iobaria. Despite the outward appearance of simplicity and plainness among its residents, Ker Sharic is known for its exceptional craftsmanship and artistry, which can rival even the most masterful artisans in grand cities like New Stetven and Oppara.

The village's artisans produce exquisite goods, including flawless window glass, delicate gossamer damask tapestries, and finely glazed stoneware. These exceptional works of art have garnered local fame and a high demand among collectors.

However, the residents of Ker Sharic are a very insular people. The villagers lead a meager and modest lifestyle and tend to remain within the confines of their community. They seldom venture beyond their village's fenced borders.

One of the key mysteries surrounding Ker Sharic is the village's enigmatic trade partners. Every month, cloaked visitors bearing a mysterious white-and-blue banner arrive in the village. These visitors purchase the village's exquisite crafts, offering unmarked gold coins in return. The income from these transactions serves as Ker Sharic's lifeblood, but the people remain in the dark about the origin and intentions of these visitors. The people are always anxious that one day these mysterious patrons might cease their monthly visits, which could disrupt the village's fragile yet steady source of income.
1027413920:
Kraggodan

11 locations:
  • Graybanks
  • Bluestone
  • Seven-Fangs Bastion
  • Felicity Vale
  • Magilloch Ranch
  • Withershin

  • Everrest
