1029033450:
Bartie's Barges

Bartie's Barges is a shipyard in The Docks run by halflings affiliated with Darabelle Fairwind's Pilot's Union. They typically build small, flat-bottomed boats.
1039039160:
Court of Seasons

The Court of Seasons is a court of nymphs that actively resists anyone coming into their domain without permission.
1048547980:
Cenotaph

The Cenotaph is an ancient, monumentally large pillar of black rock which rises from a cliff on the southern tip of the Tusk Mountains in the Hold of Belkzen. The main feature of this enormous rock is the huge pair of fifty-foot-high doors that bar entrance to whatever lies within.
1055064990:
Hope's Hollow

The quiet agricultural town of Hope's Hollow is an hour's ride along the eastern road from the city of Cassomir in Taldor, through the Blackwood Swamp.
1058361400:
Vauntil

The small town of Vauntil is located in the Ustalavic province of Caliphas on Lake Encarthan's Avalon Bay. The settlement is notable for its skilled folk artisans, fierce competition for noble patronage, flowers, and good food and wine.

The town is perhaps the most scenic and affluent regions in the county, largely due to the miles of vineyards and flower farms surrounding the settlement. Each year, chefs, vintners, perfumers, jewelers, and artists of all walks compete in the annual "Last Breath" festival for the highly coveted mantle "Worth Dying For". The competition is mired in regular tragedies and scandals including the use of human ingredients, assassinations, and mysterious deaths. Modern participants are unperturbed by this disasters, giving the competition a dangerous reputation and a morbid theme which has served to heighten its infamy.

Vauntil was the location where troops of the Shining Crusade established a beachhead in their war against Tar-Baphon in 3801 AR.
1061727580:
Botosani

The Rahadoumi city of Botosani is located on the Arcadian Ocean on the west coast of the Kingdom of Man.

As with the rest of the nation, religion is completely banned in the city but persistent rumors of a cult of Iomedae have recently gained enough credence to be considered a threat, resulting in an increase in activity among the Pure Legion. The ongoing famine besetting Botosani makes fully devoting the resources needed difficult for the Legion, however, and they now look for outside help to either rout the cult by force or to create a public spectacle which they could then clean up.

Famine and cults are not the only threats to the well-being of Botosani, though, as three times in the last half century the city has been the victim of a terrible plague and pirates from the nearby Shackles continue to make trouble for the nation's navy and trading vessels going to and from the busy port. Being the major Rahadoumi port nearest the Eye of Abendego, where the pirates often flee for refuge, it is here that able seamen and naval guides are most in demand to help track down and dispatch the seafaring threats.
1065911490:
Splitskull Fortress

Splitskull Fortress, also called Splitskull Keep, was an outpost of Koldukar before the orcs siege took it, though its name, from 7,000 years ago when it was still occupied by dwarves, has been lost to the ages. The fortress sits atop a hill and, even after so much time, remains in good condition thanks to dwarven engineering and the occasional repairs made by orcs. A stone face of a dwarf seems to watch over the area, though a past attack on the fortress left a gash that makes it appear to have a scar. The Cleft Head hold has inhabited the fortress since Grask Uldeth ordered them to watch over Freedom Town from this position and the village of Ossogard was born arround it.
1074905700:
Vyre

The decadent autonomous city of Vyre (also known as the City of Masks), is located on Vyre Island in Nisroch Bay abutting the territory of the recently independent nation of Ravounel.
1101620030:
Three-Troll

Three-Troll is a small town in the Irriseni province known as the Verge which holds a great number of ice trolls. Three-Troll began as a camp for ice trolls raiding the neighboring Grungir Forest and over time grew into a small town. Three rock troll statues stand in the town square; these were rock trolls petrified when they were caught in the sun during the Winter War and have since given the name to the town. The ruler of Three-Troll is Baroness Nedyalka but she prefers to stay in the duke's court and rarely visits the town leaving the ice trolls to govern themselves.
1102211300:
Vashnarstill Shipyard

The Vashnarstill Shipyard is a centuries-old fixture of the Yolubilis Harbor area of Kintargo. Named for House Vashnarstill, it has remained the most reliable dockyard for over 10 generations due to its management. Ownership is handed down on the basis of merit rather than blood ties; each owner has tried to select the best employee to succeed him, which has worked well so far.

Its owner as of 4724 AR was Kohl Draksitus. In 4715 AR, Draksitus' most significant concerns were the stiff competition from new yards in the rival Chelaxian cites of Westcrown and Corentyn. However, her more recent struggles are the result of keeping up with the demands of Sendi Vashnarstill, whose ambitions to make Kintargo a hub of trade with the distant continent of Arcadia are taxing the shipyard's capacity.
1109903940:
Old Temple of Erastil

The Old Temple of Erastil in Westcrown lies abandoned in the Rego Cader. It once served the lower-middle-class residents of the neighborhood, who ran simple shops and helped each other, but this spiritual center is no longer what it once was. In 4709 AR, it was used as a base of operations by a gang of cambions who were robbing and causing anarchy in the city.
1112952010:
Bookmaker

Bookmaker is the largest bookshop in all Korvosa and it attracts eager readers from across the city. Owned by the aiuvarin Costa Serimus, the books on offer within Bookmaker range from books of legends about Ancient Azlant to up-to-date academic reports on Osirion archaeology. Students from Theumanexus College, the University of Korvosa, and various other smaller schools and colleges all buy their books from Bookmaker and many of the professors of these institutions have their published works available in the store. Notably, the students of the Acadamae do not purchase their books here. Bookmaker has an informal agreement to avoid too much overlap with the bookshop Gilded Orrery: the Gilded Orrery focuses on all things arcane, while Bookmaker instead stocks titles with a more general focus on knowledge. Bookmaker sits above a hidden entrance to the maze of underground chambers known as The Vaults.
1115825740:
Estate of Yargos Gill

The Estate of Yargos Gill is the beautiful residence of one of Absalom's poorest residents, at least in the past, as he had lived in The Puddles but rose to prominence after his insightful contributions during the Black Echelon Uprising. Yargos Gill has attended numerous events with the Pathfinder Society, and no one in the city knows more about military history than he does. He is familiar with all the city's sieges, the history of the Inner Sea wars, and has published several articles specifically on the conflicts in Andoran. The estate's gardens feature many statues of military heroes from the People's Revolt, and he often shares anecdotes with visitors, as he actively participated in the conflict. The three-story stone manor house stands in the center of the garden, topped with a large statue of Talmandor, as the estate has served for decades as the residence of the ambassador of the democratic republic. The third floor is reserved for such visitors, and the first two contain his vast library and museum, which Andoran's allies can view free of charge. However, a fee must be paid if outsiders wish to see it or have him assist them in their research.
1117594980:
Canaroden

Canaroden is the longest and deepest canal in Westcrown, physically separating Parego Spera and Parego Dospera. The canal walls in the southern area feature bas-reliefs of the city's founders, while those in the north depict faces of Aroden. Thanks to the magical properties of these walls, they have remained undisturbed for centuries after their construction. In the past, eight bridges spanned the canal, though only two remain: Bladewing Bridge and Truemount Bridge.
1133139580:
Citadel Gheisteno

Citadel Gheisteno was the headquarters of the Order of the Crux, a now defunct order of mercenaries who claimed to be Hellknights. The order refused to disband after the Chelaxian Civil War and was destroyed by the Order of the Scourge in Desnus 4663 AR. The castle too was put to the torch, and it stood for over 50 years as a charred reminder to those who might oppose the Hell-inspired proponents of absolute law.

In 4688 AR, a grim parody of the former fortress reappeared along with three undead graveknights who called themselves the Order of the Crux, and began to gather an army of deathless soldiers around them.
1142637380:
Temple of Sivanah

The Temple of Sivanah of Absalom is located in the Wise Quarter, where knowledge of all sciences and practices is sought. It is a small structure that changes almost imperceptibly each day, and the students of the Arcanamirium who visit it regularly say that its artistic style also changes, although at a less noticeable pace. There is another identical temple near the College of Mysteries, with the same priests who move between locations. There are several theories as to why both are near magical institutions of learning, but none seems the most credible.
1148319470:
Wreck of the Nightvoice

The Nightvoice was a ship of exploration in the employ of the Pathfinder Society, with a home port of Almas, Andoran. The ship went missing while returning from a voyage to the coast of southern Garund decades prior to 4710 AR. Its true fate was unknown to most: it had crashed between a pair of rocks off the shore of Silent Island, a small island off the coast of Smuggler's Shiv in Desperation Bay, decades prior to 4710 AR.
1158252590:
Dreamer's Reach

The barren peak of Dreamer's Reach is also one of the highest in the area. It holds special significance for the Kellid people, who often seek divine guidance at this site. They undergo a spiritual journey to the mountain's summit, where they remove all of their clothes above the waist and wait in the harsh cold conditions for a waking dream from Desna. Barring that, they continue waiting until spirits take them away to the Boneyard.

Despite its spiritual importance, Dreamer's Reach is a place rarely visited by animals and giants, thus, the physical challenges and exposure to the elements most threaten those who make the treacherous climb. However, this location is also a place where the boundary between the Universe and the Dreamlands is thin, and people who seek visions at Dreamer's Reach might find themselves engaged in fierce battles with nightmares that become dangerously real.
1158664850:
Crystalhurst

Crystalhurst is a secretive town in the Southern Fangwood ruled by a circle of druids who make sure the homes and other structures of the town blend in with the natural landscape. The druids willingly help the Nirmathi repel Molthuni invaders, now that their previous central concern, the fight against the Darkblight, was ended with the fall of the debased fey ruler Arlantia in 4717 AR.
1160994100:
Daigaki Castle

Daigaki Castle (pronounced dai-ga-kee) is a haunted stronghold in southern Linvarre in Tian Xia, and the lair of a large group of undead led by the ghost of Tsubokandu.

After the fall of Imperial Lung Wa in 7108 IC, the city of Kamikobu—now known as New Oppara—was besieged by a dozen bandit warlords and Tsubokandu was considered the cruelest of them all. Tsubokandu was a disgraced samurai warlord from the neighbouring kingdom of Songbai who chose exile over an honourable death.

Tsubokandu was defeated by the founder of Linvarre's predecessor colony of Amanandar, General Orphyrea Amanandar, but after his death his ghost returned to Daigaki Castle and began gathering an army of the dead to reap some unknown terrible revenge.
1166075210:
Pillars of Rovagug

The Pillars of Rovagug are a set of mountains in the far north of the World's Edge Mountains in Taldor.
1170868650:
Embassy of New Thassilon

The Embassy of New Thassilon, located in the Foreign Quarter, represents the interests of Eurythnia and Edasseril in Absalom. During its first year of operation, both navion agents cooperated with their stone giant allies to renovate a dilapidated building into a magnificent embassy. The embassy is divided into three parts, the first is a central hall open to the public, with the Sihedron symbol engraved on the floor. It is here that diplomats assure everyone that their masters do not cause problems in the region. The west hall is where Edasseril's agents paint and renovate the structure, aiming to make it more magnificent, Ambassador Firandivar is found there, claiming to be one of Belimarius's grandsons. In the west hall, Eurythnian agents have created a shrine for Nocticula, and a simulacrum of Sorshen acts as ambassador, calling herself Ayandai to avoid confusion, she offers the hall to artists persecuted by the city's inhabitants.
1171987960:
Pier's End

Pier's End is a small, squalid fishing village on the north coast of the Isle of Kortos within the domain of the city-state of Absalom. It is surrounded by a hard-to-cross bog known as the Dunmire.
1172836090:
Maroux's Isle

Maroux's Isle is located in the northeastern part of the Lady's Cape, marking the last place one encounters civilization before entering the cape itself. An old trail leads to the island, which is connected by a bridge. Several boggard and xulgath heads have been placed there as warnings to the local tribes not to approach. The island contains only a hut, where the witch Maroux lives, preferring solitude and avoiding interaction with the rest of society.
1178104660:
Cypher Lodge

The Cypher Lodge in Riddleport is the headquarters of the Order of Cyphers. It is a combination of library, laboratory, research hall, and museum where cyphermages pursue the secrets of Thassilon.
1181552570:
Bail House

The Bail House is the headquarters of the Token Guard, the district watch of the Coins district of Absalom. It gets its name from the guards' habit of jailing everyone present at a disturbance and holding them until they can post bail, which is usually 1 copper piece per arrest. The edifice is a squat building of wood and mortar with barred windows and two gated entrances, designed primarily to survive a riot.
1182704060:
Sandpoint

Sandpoint is known as the 'Light of the Lost Coast' and is a small town in south-western Varisia under the influence of the city of Magnimar to its south. Sandpoint remains the only place of note along the Lost Coast, but it has grown considerably in the past few years and is now Varisia's sixth-largest settlement; nevertheless, it still maintains the air of a sleepy fishing town. Sandpoint is most famous for the arts: particularly theatre and high-quality glass-blowing.
1194346500:
Cheeho the Talking Dog

Cheeho the Talking Dog is, as the name suggests, a stall where Cheeho entertains travelers with his stories, as he can communicate like a person. The truth is, he was once a human man, but after stealing an amulet from a burial site, he was cursed to live out his days as a dog, and here he is earning money to see if he can break the curse.
1204938520:
Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters

The Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters is among Jawbone's fancy businesses, drawing attention for being a bare, undecorated building. The guild members have no interest in participating in local entertainment; their job is to guide people who are using the city's canals and are interested in what the city can offer them.
1226274680:
Steelhead Pier

The Steelhead Pier is always bustling with people embarking or disembarking, making it the most busy pier in all of Greengold. It's traditional to name piers after freshwater fish, and the Steelhead's and the other piers share similar names.
1233016910:
Gutblood Labyrinth

The Gutblood Labyrinth is located in the Rechiend's Plains, in a section of mostly featureless grassland west of Free Station in the western Mwangi Expanse. It is beneath a fissure so abrupt that many unwary travellers have nearly fallen into it. It is so treacherous that the locals know it as "Calistria's Grin".

Those who descend into the fissure find nothing for hundreds of feet until reaching depths where the sun is but a memory. In its deepest reaches, a series of disconnected caverns dot both sides of the fissure and wind off in every direction for miles.

Though once part of the region of the Nar-Voth known as the Sleepless Realm of Reguare, the Gutblood Labyrinth was cut off from the wider region for reasons unknown. The only hint at what the ancient Ghol-Gani cyclops might have been trying to contain is in the bodies of those who try to explore the Gutblood Labyrinth's depths. Within weeks Their corpses inevitably wash up along the coast of the Fever Sea bloated and mutilated almost beyond recognition.
1263388440:
Wreck of the Brine Demon

1 locations:
  • Scallywag
1264076070:
Anuli

Anuli, also known as 'The City of New Beginnings', is the northernmost city of the celestially empowered nation of Holomog, a distant nation in southern Garund, and is close to the southern border of Geb.
1267948070:
Saucy Wench

The Saucy Wench meadhall is one of the very few of the permanent buildings in the Grand Bazaar in the Coins district of Absalom. The popular four-story edifice near the center of the bazaar serves roast meat, bread, and mead, and is well-known for its uniformed all-female waiting staff. The tavern's employees all work for owner Lady Kythes Finch, captain of the Token Guard, so unwelcome advances are often dealt with by one of the many off-duty guardsmen who number among the meadhall's patrons.
1274597420:
Cascina

The tiny hamlet of Cascina is a sleepy little settlement located in Meratt County in the Tandak Prefecture south of the Verduran Forest and east of Cassomir. Cascina consists of only a few extended families who once worked for the Voinum barony most of whom now live as simple farmers of goats, sheep, or wheat. The hamlet's most notable resident is Yarelle Towsine, a minor wizard who offers her spells to help the local community. As part of Meratt County, Cascina is subjected to the harsh rule of House Lotheed.
1278893340:
Horse Shop

The Horse Shop is, as its name suggests, a no-frills livery stable and equestrian supply shop that offers fair prices. It is located in the East Shore neighborhood of the Varisian city of Korvosa.
1279085590:
Bafra's Face

Bafra's Face is an ancient stone sculpture located on a stony hillside in the Verduran Province of Taldor. Its construction was ordered by Grand Prince Bafra, who ruled the empire in the late 1st millennium AR. The face was carved to depict Bafra overlooking the Tandak Plains, but as of 4717 AR it had become too heavily eroded to make out any of its details.

As of 4717 AR the statue was the site of a spriggan-led clan attempting to raise its own stone deity in order to stave off the Universe's horrors and return to the First World. The clan's leader is the spriggan oracle Ifgeiher, whose plan involves transforming Bafra's Face into a stone colossus, and his followers—which gargoyles and other creatures—already worship the statue as a "rock god". A small clan of moss trolls have also occupied the site but do not participate in Ifgeiher's faith.
1285233900:
Kashang

Kashang is a forbidden, ancient ruin of the long-extinct Valashai Empire that is located in the Chenlun Mountains' eastern foothills, in the depths of the deadly Valashmai Jungle. Kashang is a series of many-stepped temples shrouded by the surrounding jungle in utter silence, and no beast from the surrounding jungles dares intrude on Kashang's unholy quiet.

Kashang's buildings are made from dark stone that is eerily clammy to the touch. Whatever horrors the ancient Valashai summoned to aid their long-gone empire still lurks here, waiting for any explorers to upset the delicate magic of the ancient rituals that bind them to the ruins. However, the lore and secrets that lurk within are enough to still draw reckless knowledge seekers to the ruins. Those who seek the foreboding ruins risk paying a terrible price for the forbidden knowledge hidden within silent Kashang.
1290931010:
Daralathyxl's Lair

Daralathyxl's Lair lies in the foothills of the mountains, in a central area where the mighty cinder dragon commands the respect and allegiance of all his children and dragons for hundreds of miles around. His legend rivals that of the Whispering Tyrant, but with the dwarves, and several tavern brawls and debates have arisen about whether the cities of the Five Kings Mountains could stop him in a rampage. When Daralathyxl stopped sending a representative to the Gathering Council, he was presumed dead, and several adventurers have gone in search of his lair, only one returning, saying that the dragon is still in perfect condition or stronger that the legends say.
1291282920:
Queen Peak

Queen Peak is the eighth-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 14,831 feet. It is located in the southern parts of the range between Mount Gustus and Mount Onik.
1327673620:
Babschu-Jong

Babschu-Jong is a major city in the samsaran country of Zi Ha on the continent of Tian Xia. It is located on a southern peak in the Kelsang Mountains and is a major trade connection on the route between Linvarre's capital of New Oppara and Zi Ha's nominal capital of Sangpo-Jong. Primary trade materials include artwork, gems, herbs, incense, livestock, and stone from Zi Ha, which are traded for grain, iron, and textiles.
1331827500:
Witchbole

The Witchbole is a giant, twisted thorn tree which Treerazer uses as a fortress and palace. As if its appearance were not harrowing enough, the bloated, gnarled tree is completely mobile, crawling slowly through the forest on its writhing roots. The Witchbole's true nature remains a mystery, though the arboreal regents of the Fierani Forest avoid the topic, leading some to believe that the massive demonic arbor was one of their kind before being corrupted to Treerazer's evil will.

The shattered crest of the Witchbole sometimes twists into the outline of a face, although this visage is vague. On its branches are the impaled corpses of those elves who sought to confront Treerazer directly.

In 4725 AR as part of his offensive Treerazer teleported the Witchbole straight to Iadara where he began a ritual to grow in power and destroy the city. Heroes where able to infiltrate the structure and the ripples of magical power within destroyed the tree making it an empty husk now.
1356125420:
Statue of Primarch Harvi

The Statue of Primarch Harvi, located in the Petal District, is one of the city's most famous public artworks, but not for a positive reason as it's considered the most ugly statue in all of Absalom. It depicts this Primarch from the Age of Enthronement on horseback during the Red Siege of 4499 AR, but his face makes him look more like he has a cold or intestinal problems than anything that inspires courage. The Conclave of Flowers seems to agree that it should be removed, but the problem is what to put in its place. Some want a statue of Lord Gyr and his fellow adventurers, including Lord Avid, of course. Others, associated with the Citizens' League, prefer a statue honoring the soldiers who fell in the city's many sieges. And the new nobles want to improve their reputation with the Vault of Abadar by erecting a statue of Abadar.
1373905010:
Jackal's Maw

Even amongst the towering peaks and deep canyons of the Brazen Peaks the Jackal's Maw dwarfs all others. It is said to be near bottomless, and its sides are sheer with only a few well-hidden trails descending into the darkness. Few creatures are foolish enough to venture down into the depths of the Jackal's Maw, and even fewer return.

In truth, its depths lead into the Darklands. The site is considered sacred by nearby Lamashtan kholo tribes who think it represents both the mouth and the womb of their patron, the mother of monsters, for the maw both devours all that falls into it and releases horrible monsters from its depth. These kholo throw regular offerings and live sacrifices screaming into the pit, and the bones of the sacrifices litter its floor and attract all manner of Darkland creatures. Few creatures are foolish enough to venture down into the depths of the Jackal's Maw, and even fewer return.
1394018700:
Highdelve

Highdelve is a settlement built by House Garess in the Golushkin Mountains in Western Brevoy.
1395423360:
Cutter Canyon

The Cutter Canyon is located east of the Mindspin Mountains on the Deepcut River. The canyon is overrun with bandits, bugbears, xulgaths, and minotaurs who make strongholds in the caverns and caves that dot the canyon. The adult crystal dragon Calcatrine has his hoard somewhere in the tunnels that connect the caverns.
1402918480:
Calladels

Calladels is located near the Sellen River on the border with both Andoran and Galt. The wet, watery environment makes the village seem like a place primarily dedicated to fishing and rural life, but it serves as a base of operations for the Brazen Bravos, who use the river to conduct their smuggling operations throughout the region.
1404904460:
Shouk

The Shouk is a tribe of degenerate ogrekin that once were human holy men. Their lair is in the Barrier Wall mountains, roughly south-east of the Thuvian city of Duwwor. Once the Shouk were holy men and oracles of some renown. Their wisdom was sought by many other nomadic tribes. However, at some point in the distant past, this clan of holy men became tainted by the blood of ogres and ogrekin began to be born among the ranks of the Shouk. With each generation the taint spread, and more and more brutish, vile ogrekin were born. Now the tribe is composed exclusively of ogrekin, and has lost all prophetic ability. Purely out of tradition, the tribes of the area still occasionally seek out the Shouk in times of great need. These visits rarely yield any result other than the loss of life, as the Shouk are also now cannibals.
1420788990:
Railford

Railford is a small settlement located on a small wooded hill a few miles inland from the larger settlement of Golsifar, on the Inner Sea coast of Taldor. The settlement is in the prefecture of Moda.
1426386750:
Tourondel Marches

The Tourondel Marches surround the Tourondel River and mark the northern border of Nirmathas. The earliest settlements in the area date back to the Shining Crusade era, and several ruins can be found in the region, which is populated by orcs and undead who sometimes harass travelers. Those who reside in the area have developed skills as merchants and tend to have a more formal demeanor compared to their southern compatriots, likely based on their historical proximity to the lawful nation of Lastwall.
1430370530:
Sothis

The Osirian capital city of Sothis is located just south of the River Sphinx delta, and is the largest, and most prosperous city in the kingdom of Osirion. The city is built around the molted husk of Ulunat, a colossal beetle that was one of the spawn of Rovagug and slain by Ancient Osirion's first pharaoh, Azghaad. Except for a short time under the Four Pharaohs of Ascension, it was also the Osirian capital city before the millennia of Keleshite rule.
1437727330:
Palace Mortales

In the southern deserts of the country of Katapesh, a giant, curving ribcage of a beast rises from the sand. Over the years, travelers have used the giant ribcage as shelter. Sometimes they refer to it as Palace Mortales.
1438069840:
Urgir

7 locations:
  • Black Tower
  • Great Palace
  • Halls of the Closed Fist
  • Market District
  • Plaza of Sky
  • Sharpened Axe
  • Table of Ralock
1438430210:
Starspine Manor

Starspine Manor is a magnificent residence that would dwarf the surrounding houses were it not for its beautiful gardens. Lord Synarr Daidalos owns the manor, which appeared overnight between two adjacent residences, though this is only a rumor. The spellcaster has purchased the property and uses it for his lavish galas. He has promised that every seventeenth of Erastus he will hold a gala more magnificent than the last, inviting the city's nobles to attend.
1463877310:
Sea Wrack Chapel

2 locations:
  • F & M's Exotic Meats
  • Latchmin's Folly
1490643180:
Taranik House

Taranik House is a large mansion in the Rego Scripa section of the Chelaxian city of Westcrown, which serves as the city's garrison for the Hellknight Order of the Rack.
1493962010:
Vannisaria

Vannisaria is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. Vannisaria was founded by Brutus Vannisar and has since been home to multiple generations of the Vannisar family, including General Jiayi Vannisar, Brutus's great-granddaughter and Linvarre's leader as of 4724 AR. The city is also host to the Temple of the Eternal and Exquisite Rose, the largest temple to Shelyn on the continent.
1497037300:
Stone of Tears

The mythical Stone of Tears was once a singular structure within a Ghol-Gani city that is now in ruins on the Dahak's Tooth island of the Shackles. The Stone of Tears lies sideways, having fallen from its ornate pedestal near the city's temple. It is said that the Stone of Tears was a cage for a beautiful nymph who long opposed the empire of Ghol-Gan and was imprisoned. Should she be released, her knowledge could reveal valuable secrets of the lost civilization.
1523912010:
Bundózo

Bundózo is a town located on the northern bank of the Lake of the Blessed Prince in the central part of Xopatl. Its economy depends on diving expeditions into a massive underwater keep, which was home to a hero-god who chose to submerge it upon his ascension. The fort seems to be dozens of storeys tall, and has yet to be fully scoured of its treasures.
1544322940:
Visheksrad

The largest city on the Crown of the World, Visheksrad is the capital of the dwarven nation of Zavaten Gura. The iron citadel is warmed by powerful coal forges, and is where the nation's ruler, Utograf Voislav, makes his home. Visheksrad is located on the coast of the Shining Sea.
1558775150:
Zhenbarghua

Zhenbarghua is the westernmost kuru settlement in the Cannibal Isles and maybe the least dedicated to the Blood Queen. Kuru from Zhenbarghua sometimes manage to escape their depraved culture and islands in search of a new life.
1559723070:
Adeletti

Adeletti is a huge warehouse used by the city's ferrymen to exchange stories, relax, and teach the younger crews the tricks of the trade. Because part of the building is above water, they often bring their barges, which they tuck under the building itself.
1568050100:
Kennel

The Kennel appears to be an unremarkable taxidermy shop, in a state of disrepair similar to other buildings in the district, but it's used by the Family Dogs criminal gang, named for all the taxidermied dogs found in the establishment's basement. The members have moved the dogs around, posing them as if they were people, which has increased the shop's reputation. Although none of the members yet know how to taxidermy, some are trying to learn. The leader, Dras, keeps the "pack" in line, and some of his members want to expand their activities into racketeering. They've already demonstrated what happens to those who don't pay, sealing their mouths shut and creating a legend about a monster that can only be scared away by taxidermied figurines.
1571568520:
Jainai

Jainai is a sarangay city on the southern portion of Bukorang island in Minata.
1586452730:
Barque Bazaar

The Barque Bazaar is a floating business where several barges have joined together, and merchants sell their foreign goods to customers all along the Absalom coast. Created by Market Master Annavi, she believed her business would be a success, but she hadn't anticipated that the residents of The Puddles, The Docks, and Precipice Quarter would be the poorest in the city, and she's struggling to keep the business afloat, both literally and figuratively. Her best customers are in The Puddles, where she's become involved with criminal gangs, acting as a fencer. However, she may be too deeply involved in the criminal underworld, and someone might eventually retaliate against her and the floating business.
1594816800:
Bergemonte

Bergemonte is a Galtan lumberjack settlement bordering the Boarwood close to Isarn. The town takes its name from the Beast of Bergemonte, which is said to have killed a large number of travelers in the area between the years of 4696 AR and 4699 AR.
1597832730:
Shadeglass Foundry

Shadeglass Foundry is Pangolais's main producer of shadowglass, using its location near the Usk River to facilitate the transport of the wood used in its creation. Art pieces made with this glass are highly prized by collectors and can also be used as materials for shadow-related spells.
1629570810:
Slave Trenches of Hakotep

The Slave Trenches of Hakotep are a series of large earthworks in Osirion built by Pharaoh Hakotep I between the Barrier Wall mountains and the headwaters of the Crook River, near the Kho-Rarme Pass. The earthworks run in strange geometric shapes across 17 square miles of desert, only from above can their true form be appreciated as the trenches form an ancient Osirian Hieroglyph that roughly translates as "Let Earth call down and bind the Sky".

The trenches' depths vary from only a few feet to fifty feet at its deepest but averages around thirty feet, their width can also vary widely but on average is around fifty feet. The trenches are often filled with drifts of sand and the bones of the slaves who died in their thousands carving them. The walls of the trench are dotted with some thousands of obelisks in various states of erosion and disrepair, about half of which are still intact. Each once housed a bound elemental, normally earth or air but sometimes fire, and the trenches still buzz with this mystic energy.

The trenches also feature towering menhirs made of polished white stone that have weathered the millennia much better than some of the surrounding stonework. The eleven menhirs are each devoted to one of the Osirian pantheon deities: Osiris, Horus, Isis, Thoth, Khepri, Ptah, Maat, Sekhmet, Ra, Sobek, and Set. The trenches also house several underground complexes which few have ever survived to explore. According to an Osiriontologist known as the Sand Sage, a tower-sized panel of hieroglyphics composed of the purest amber, also known as the Amber Chronograph, is located deep within the Slave Trenches. These hieroglyphs are numbers which are slowly counting down.

The trenches are inhabited by a unique form of undead called ossumentals that are found nowhere else. The trenches' inhabitants include Kenjutret, an immortal roc that once served as the loyal pet of Hakotep I as well as a sphinx obsessed with the mystery of the trenches. The area surrounding the Slave Trenches of Hakotep is known to be home to a variety of undead and elementals, rift drakes, a tribe of ash giants far from their home in the Mana Wastes and even a phoenix.
1630955130:
Lost Lodge

Lost Lodge is a secret but forgotten Desnan safehouse in Nidal's southeastern Uskwood Forest that contains supplies, weapons, and relics of the Song of Spheres, but is surrounded by a nest of deathwebs, placed there by Shades of the Uskwood druids centuries ago.
1636583800:
Azure Star

7 locations:
  • Aandrik the Chirurgeon
  • Ahkmed's Carpets
  • Council Hall and Gaol Block
  • Jangley's Shop
  • Marble Sphinx
  • Temple of Nethys
  • The Ramps
1639632460:
Kenabres

Kenabres is a city that borders the Sarkoris Scar, formerly known as the Worldwound. Its near-destruction in 4713 AR during a demonic assault was the inciting event of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade that eventually resulted in the Worldwound's closure.
1644677570:
Dragonfen

The Dragonfen is a wetland in central Taldor. Centuries ago it was the domain of the great wyrm black dragon Aeteperax, until the creature was killed by Lady Tula Belhaim.
1658835580:
Solitary Pool

The Solitary Pool is a small oasis and caravanserai in the Meraz Desert of Qadira. It is operated by Razinia, who appears to visitors disguised as a jann but is secretly a ghul.
1665242580:
Pezzack

Pezzack is a town in western Cheliax on the Sallow Coast of the Arcadian Ocean. Nestled in Devil's Perch among the Menador Mountains, the isolated town has long been a seedbed for sedition against the infernal government far way in Egorian. The crown has responded with several naval blockades and year-long sieges that have left the settlement quite isolated from the rest of the country, and moved the seat of the Archduchy of Hellcoast from Pezzack to Belde.
1665471310:
Red Pyramid

2 locations:
  • Ruined Monastery
  • Sisters of the Quill
1675359790:
Arzikal

Also known as the City of Hungry Spires, the ruined city of Arzikal is located in the Mwangi Expanse's Bandu Hills, overlooking the Screaming Jungle.

The city appears as if an angry god ripped up the walls and buildings and stacked them on top of each other to form hundreds of precarious spires. It seems as if each spire could fall at any moment, and some do—yet the next day the fallen spire stands once more, but not necessarily in its previous location.

Strangely, sounds from the nearby jungle do not carry into the ruins. The only noise is the creaking of the towers.

The stone surface of the city's floor is marked by countless handprints, varying from the size of a human child's hand to massive prints over twenty feet across. All of these hand prints have seven fingers.

Those who dare to spend the night in the city are killed by some unknown force, their crushed bodies and scattered belongings added to the spires.
1696495470:
Biston

Among ancient cliff-side structures (rumored to have once housed a long-extinct tribe of harpies) in caves along the shore of Lake Syrantula lies the town of Biston. It was founded in 4574 AR by Jhenni Biston, who cleared the tunnels of their xulgath inhabitants.

While citizens of Korvosa, the farmers and fishermen of Biston have long held a strong sense of independence, and in 4658 AR Jhenni's grandson Randas Biston led a group of separatists in a revolt to cede the town from Korvosan control. The government responded in full force, sending half the Sable Company and an entire regiment of Korvosan Guards to maintain order. All of the rebels were executed in Korvosa, and martial law was maintained in Biston until 4690 AR when it was lifted by King Eodred II.
1696934130:
Spire of Lemriss

The Spire of Lemriss, also called the Green Spire of Lemriss, is a tree-like growth hundreds of feet in height located in western Varisia on the border between the Fogscar Mountains and the Churlwood. Its core is composed of braided and twisted wooden trunks, surrounded by arm-deep shell of vines. Countless birds and forest creatures make their home within its near-vertical structure, their chirps and hoots heard for miles around. Some believe that the spire is a cutting from the World Tree, although this has never been proven.
1709775230:
Fiendslair

Fiendslair is one of the Towers of the Fiendlorn constructed to serve experiments toward immortality conducted by Mesandroth Fiendlorn. Related to Fiendlorn's experiments with reverse possession of a demon and forms of controlled undeath, it was sealed and abandoned after the assistant tasked with its maintenance, Eledwyn, attempted and failed to betray Fiendlorn. It is located in the Umbral Basin northeast of Ridwan.

An expedition to Fiendslair in 4714 AR, involving agents of the Aspis Consortium and rebels against House Thrune's rule over Cheliax in pursuit of a nightblade, resulted in a catastrophic failure.
1717012220:
Baghava

Baghava is one of the mahajanapadas that make up the sacred region of the Divine Garden at the eastern edge of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. The city-state is located amongst mangrove forests on the region's southern coastline of the Embaral Ocean.

Baghava is renowned for the huge temple and ashram complexes across its holdings and its vast beautiful gardens, all of which are guarded by minotaur-priests of Gaelata.
1726451930:
Heidmarch Manor

3 locations:
  • Derexhi Villa
  • House of Lords
  • Lord-Mayor's Menagerie
1731120820:
Jeggare Light

2 locations:
  • Endrin Military Academy
  • Old City Hall
1739836940:
Observatory

A 20-foot-square stone platform in the middle of an otherwise featureless plain in southern Katapesh, the Observatory would be of little interest if not for its spectacular magic.

The platform's craftsmanship indicates that it is of dwarven workmanship. In the centre is carved an ornate butterfly, the religious symbol of Desna, while in each corner of the platform stands a pillar. The site is clearly dedicated to the goddess of dreams and travel, and when a person lies on her religious symbol and looks up at the sky, it is vastly magnified. Between the four corner pillars, the night sky appears much closer and clearer, stars pulse, and distant meteors fly past as if mere feet above.

It is no surprise this site is seen as holy and attracts worshippers, astronomers, and prophets all seeking inspiration.
1742175740:
Whirlpool Rush

The Whirlpool Rush is a seaside cataract at the Nalt's Island in the Shackles. It is located on the eastern end of the straits between Redbow Isle and Nalt's Island. Two sibling charybdises are said to be the cause of the perpetual vortex beneath the cataract. Sailors trying to avoid the hostile sedacthy-plagued waters of Shark Island, dare the straits between Redbow Isle and Nalt's Island and often meet their doom beneath the crashing waves of the whirlpool.
1745863760:
Jayat-Von

Jayat-Von is the largest city in the northern region of Vudra known as the Crying Jungle and is that region's capital city. The Crying Jungle is one of the densest, most verdant jungles in all of Casmaron, and like the rest of the region the city is overgrown with jungle foliage.
1764014200:
Mivon

The city of Mivon is the capital of the kingdom of Mivon, the only large settlement in the area and the seat of government. It is a walled city built at the confluence of two major rivers: one reaching to Restov (the Shrike River) and the other to New Stetven. Its stone walls have protected the city from a number of sieges, but their foundations constantly sink into the marsh that surrounds the city. The walls also contain granaries and livestock pens.

The great Council Hall stands in the center of the town, and is itself surrounded by stronger walls. The market square surrounds the Council Hall. Most of the buildings are made of wood and are no more than two stories high. Most visitors that are just passing through, stay in the ramshackle huts of the lower quarter. Half the houses here are brothels, gambling dens, drug parlors, and worse. Three different local gangs, the Fast Fallen, the River's Edge Cutters, and the Half-Deads fight for control over the quarter.

The main square of the city of Mivon hosts a weekly gathering, called the Sevier, for swordsmen aged 16 to 60 who wish to duel in public and improve their reputation.

The nine great Aldori Houses and their holdings create a wide ring around the city. Each of the larger families has constructed its own keep of stone or wood on the hills.

The city has a large fishing industry that specialises in catching eels in the pools surrounding the city. It is said that many of the eels have tasted human flesh due to the violent nature of the city, and the phrase "a walk to the fishponds" has a very sinister meaning as the person normally does not return.
1782787010:
Skrata

Skrata is a small town on the Rimeflow River, in the westernmost Irriseni province called the Verge that acts as a waystation of goods shipped to the capital of Whitethrone from the north, through the Iceflow River, and as a supply depot for the army. Skrata's docks are always busy, and its warehouses are full with goods destined for the army or the Rimeflow route.
1786838590:
Hor-Aha

The great lighthouse of Hor-Aha is located on a small island north of the Coast of Graves in Osirion. During the height of ancient Osirion, the lighthouse was a key navigation aid. Its magical light not only marked the location but also gave information about the upcoming weather. Hor-Aha has remained dark for thousands of years, but recently lurid red and purple light strobes from its lantern room, the sign of violent gales. The few vessels that have since made landfall on the small isle were never heard from again.
1799810740:
Xer

Xer (pronounced ZER) is an important port on the banks of Lake Encarthan and the Glass River. It serves as one of the two main ports for all Razmiran, and much of the trade goods that flow from across the lake are funnelled through Xer. The importance of Xer means it is tightly controlled by the priests of Razmir, who collect tithes from every business in Xer—since anyone who lives in Xer must be a worshiper of Razmir, they collect from everyone.

Unfortunately for those who visit the city, the priests are not particular about whom they demand tithes from, and happily "indoctrinate" any foreign sailor or merchant with the tenets of Razmir's religion until they agree to the "willing donation" asked of them. Even worse, this sort of behaviour is not just limited to those who walk the streets of Xer. Faith barges piloted by Razmir's masked priests sail the surrounding waters, boarding the ships of those who are not making port in Xer to spread the word of their faith (generally by bullying non-believers) and collect tithes (by demanding tribute to pass).

Rumour suggests that because Xer was the first city Razmir conquered when he came to the area, some of its inhabitants remember certain secrets that the faithful would deem blasphemy, including the fact that Xer might be home to a number of bastard offspring of the Living God.
1811583610:
Ravenmoor

The Varisian village of Ravenmoor is a holding of Magnimar located on the shores of the Lampblack River, well off the main roads connecting Magnimar and Riddleport.
1827545120:
Cho-Tzu

Cho-Tzu is the main settlement on the Shenchu Bay island in the Shackles. It is built around a large triangular harbor.

Most of the harbor is used by the Shenchu pirate vessels and the merchants ships. A second city is formed in the remaining part of the harbor consisting of 50 or more junks, rafts, and other ramshackle watercraft occupied by over 200 Tian-Sings, descended from sailors from Zo Piaobo, the floating city of Minata. These folk rarely set foot on dry land and are known to provide services from fortune-telling to midwifery but can also be hired as thugs. Most of the inhabitants of Cho-Tzu are Tian-Sings but the ramshackle neighborhoods on the northernmost side of the city are populated by tengu who escaped from Imperial Lung Wa long ago.
1827619490:
Antoll

Antoll is the former capital of Iobaria, serving that role between 3304 AR and the fall of New Iobaria in 4659 AR.
1849259480:
Melfesh

The Varisian town of Melfesh straddles both the northern and southern shores of the Yondabakari River as it flows into Lake Syrantula. Built on long piers that stick out into the river, it is was founded by Jorsh Melfesh in 4569 AR.
1850273150:
Cauldrons of Calm and Calamity

1 locations:
  • Mount Keeba
1856015650:
Ganderhal's Lair

Ganderhal's Lair is an underground vault on the Chelaxian side of the Menador Mountains, where the mountains form the border between Cheliax and Nidal. The lair is north of the Doracium River and south of the Nidalese settlement of Ridwan.
1866898120:
Rhuvasi

Rhuvasi was a dwarven settlement whose inhabitants were forced out by the dwarves of Taggoret after the Battle of Scattered Ash.
1871387880:
Xopatl Mountain

Xopatl Mountain is located at the centre of the Arcadian nation of Xopatl and is all that remains of the mountains that once covered the entire nation. Xopatl was formed after a massive fragment of the Aeon Star struck Arcadia in the Mildanesi Mountains at the start of the Age of Darkness. The hero-gods of Xopatl found this shard and, after throwing off the yoke of the Razatlan Empire, destroyed the mountains to create the area that is modern Xopatl. Xopatl Mountain is the sole remaining mountain, left looming in place at the nation's centre.

Xopatl Mountain is a massive volcano, but the hero-gods enchanted it so that it would never erupt unless given a specific command, the idea being that the hero-gods could use this should Xopatl ever completely fall to invaders. Xopatl Mountain now has 10,000 years of magma built up below it, and none left alive know what the magical command is that would cause Xopatl Mountain to erupt.
1877557110:
Clockwork Cathedral

6 locations:
  • Eternal Shackle
  • Javelin Gallery
  • Misery Row
  • Prophet's Academy
  • Taurean Embassy
  • Temple of Lost Coins
1888145540:
Hero's Rest

Hero's Rest, in the Hagreach region of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, is a memorial to honor the most famous of those who have died defending Hagreach from the Irriseni raiders and whose bodies have not been recovered, remaining where they were slain across the border in Irrisen. All Hero's Rest cairns and graves are empty and the memorial is also known as the Empty Boneyard. The hilltop memorial which overlooks the eastern border is protected by a silver dragon named Avaleru. The dragon has no true love for the violent Ulfen neighbors, but she hates the Irriseni witches and their agents. It is said that Avaleru uses a part of the regular yearly tithes she gets as a guardian to grant magical boons to kind and good-mannerly Ulfen, assuming the form of a mysterious silver-haired woman.

The hill of Hero's Rest is located on the western bank of the Iceflow River, 50 miles north of Blackraven Hall and opposite τhe Irriseni towns of Yensa and Soduras.
1888618020:
Gecko

3 locations:
  • Eyes of the Hawk
  • Old Fang
  • Shrine of Sarenrae
1909869720:
Arcanamirium

The Arcanamirium is not only the largest and oldest school in Absalom, but quite possibly the most prestigious arcane magic academy in the entire Inner Sea region of Golarion.
1918522980:
Mirnbay

First founded in 1528 AR, Mirnbay is a vibrant and prosperous port city. Protected by palisades and located on a peaceful lagoon of the Castrovin Sea near the mouth of the Myrfrus River, Mirnbay is the largest and most peaceful city in the land of Iobaria.

Along with several other nearby humanoid settlements, Mirnbay was sacked in 1900 AR by the united centaur tribes of the Caemorin, under the banner of Errindayn the Seer. Mirnbay was later refounded in 2108 AR with its defences significantly strengthened, and centaurs were banned from the city for the next 400 years. With New Iobaria's collapse in 4659 AR, Mirnbay became one of last remaining power centres in the region.

Mirnbay's 22 noble houses rule the port city through a labyrinthine political system based on careful alliances and strategies. This system has faced disruption from the recent influx of Galtan nobility fleeing that nation's revolution. As of 4710 AR, Galtans controlled more than a third of the city's trade guilds, as well as five of the 22 noble houses. The city is led by an alliance of House Rhukov, a breakaway faction of the Iobarian royal House Arjal that controls the wizards of the city's arcane college, and House Xsagi, which controls Mirnbay's military.
1929825840:
Soduras

Soduras is a small fishing community on the Iceflow River, which forms the border between the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch and the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach. Due to its location, it is frequently used as a stopover and resupply point for raiding parties of ice trolls, witchwargs, or mercenary bands conducting cross-border raids.

As of 4713 AR, the town's ruler Baroness Vjera encourages the martial visitors and orders the townsfolk to provide housing, food, and entertainment to them. Soduras' north end houses a large number of inns, as well as fighting pits that cater to ice trolls and witchwargs. Human mercenary companies usually stay in the south end of the town and enjoy themselves at the brothels of Madame Plana Vectenstyn. Life is hard for Soduras' residents, but these hardships have also inspired tough adventurers.
1931162120:
Flesk

Flesk is a settlement on the central eastern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom.
1936573510:
Liclac

The ruined city of Liclac was once a thriving settlement of the now-extinct nation of Rastel. The ruins stand upon the banks of the Upper Korir River on the edge of the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse. Just as with all other ruins of the Rastel culture, Liclac shows signs of fire, conflict, and cannibalism.
1942359260:
Well of Gorum

The Well of Gorum was formed when a piece of his armor struck Almas, thankfully only destroying a couple of empty warehouses, and the metal was recovered by the city guard. After the storms, the crater has filled with water, and a patrol anxiously watches the area.
1943297600:
Guild of Wonders

The headquarters of the Guild of Wonders is ostensibly an unmarked, poorly received tourism attraction composed of creative taxidermy that purportedly depicts cryptids and other beings, and is located in a shoddy building in the Westgate neighborhood of Absalom. However, its decrepit appearance masks its true purpose as a guildhouse for assassins, spies, and saboteurs.
1957901640:
Starfall

Starfall is a city just as brutal and as harsh as the unforgiving Numerian land that it is capital of. The place is a mockery of the soft capitals of the south, with a level of decadence that would even put Taldor to shame. This decadence is surrounded by the filthy reek of human misery. Here the Black Sovereign, currently Kevoth-Kul, rules utterly unchallenged, his every whim catered to, while the rest of city suffers under his harsh rule. Until recently the only people who benefitted were those who help the court of the Black Sovereign indulge in his carnal decadence.
1961565530:
Maashinelle

Maashinelle is the largest settlement in Lambreth and serves as the seat of power for its dictator, Lord Kamdyn Arnefax. The town is located in the lowlands where the Tolemaida River joins the West Sellen River and has a large dockside along the water.
1962457120:
Minderhal's Anvil

Minderhal's Anvil is an ancient temple to the giant smith-god Minderhal located in the Iron Peaks on the shores of the Storval Deep. Built thousands of years ago in the Thassilonian nation of Shalast with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants, the temple is dominated by enormous, cracked marble pillars, a gigantic forge-altar, and a stone statue of Minderhal seated on his throne looking out over the Storval Deep with his chin resting on his fist. Few modern giants visit this old site, and the immense furnace, traditionally fed by the bodies of convicted criminals, stands cold.
1963092150:
Vaneo Arvanxi

7 locations:
  • Arodennama
  • Delvehaven
  • Rolan's Shop
  • Sanqatada Cinqarda
  • Shrine of Aroden

1967189110:
Silken Court

The Silken Court is a brothel in the Petal District of Absalom. It is the city's most expensive establishment of its type, and among of the Inner Sea's most famous. Previously owned and managed by Elissa the Fair until her recent retirement, it has since been fully renovated by its new manager, the Calistrian Head Hetaera Dyrianna of House Avenstar.
1988953080:
Ajitesh Valley

Ajitesh Valley is a holy site in the north-western corner of the Divine Garden region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. The valley is a natural elephant graveyard where the beasts go to die.

In 4465 AR, the Vile Prince Rajput Shivji Shashibhushan, a powerful necromancer, violated this sacred site to harvest its vast supply of bone and ivory and build undead known as rajput ambari. He also used the materials for his blasphemous Palace of Ivory and Bone.
1989005130:
Dawnfoot

The small town of Dawnfoot lies just southeast of the city of Absalom, between the city walls surrounding the Precipice Quarter and Starwatch Keep. It began as a camp for members of the Starwatch (who must live outside the city walls due to an ancient agreement with the First Guard), but has since grown into a somewhat insular community of military families, many of whom can look back on ten generations who have served in the Starwatch. Those families who do not have members in the Starwatch generally work in some other support capacity in Starwatch Keep. Its city hall is housed in a converted siege engine.
1992463930:
Lod

Lod is a small Irriseni town near the headwaters of the Marbleflow River in Hoarwood province.

Lod is the first stop of the trade path north along the Marbleflow River. The town welcomes Varisian merchants and its river front is full of wharves and warehouses. A dry dock called Cold Queen Waters repairs damaged barges and also crafts and sells small riverboats. Seven inns are close to the docks serving the merchants, riverboat captains, and their crews. The Boar's Head inn is renowned for its attached brothel and gambling house, run by the siblings Grevel and Holz Nabotov. Other known inns in the docks are the Sister Helena's and the Warm Doxy.

The wealthy widowed owner of half a dozen warehouses, Vilma Lochnik is secretly a member of the underground anti-government movement Heralds of Summer's Return. Vilma is efficient in smuggling refugees out of Irrisen.

Baroness Arisa is the ruler of the town of Lod. She has tried to open an overland trade path between Lod and the provincial capital of Hoarwood through the forest but she cannot deal with a strangely well-organized group of bandits called the Bitter Brotherhood.
1994373900:
Enganoka

Enganoka is the capital of Hosekikozan Province located on the western coast of Minkai and a significant port for trade. It is particularly well known for the luxury goods—especially dyes, gemstones, and silk—that it trades out of its Kuroi Lane market.
2002465600:
Blooder's Bend

Blooder's Bend is a dried-up riverbed in an area of ​​the Fangwood where tall trees block sunlight and where the Chernasardo Rangers have maintained a presence with some bases in the past.
2004143240:
Solskinn

Solskinn is a small town in the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor, located to the south of Grungir Forest and to the north-east of Southmoor's capital of Jol.

As of 4711 AR it was officially ruled by Southmoor's Linnorm King Opir Eightfingers, but local leaders would take control whenever they felt that they could. Pleasant during the day, the small town was under forced curfew at night, as anyone leaving their homes is abducted by the visiting shadows.

Eightfingers went missing after attempting an ill-fated series of raids against New Thassilon in 4719 AR, vacating his throne. His successor was Ostog the Unslain. The town's relationship with Ostog is unknown.
2020467780:
Whiterook

Whiterook, sometimes denoted White Rook, is located in the east of Southmoor in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings hard against the border with Irrisen, from which nation the people of Whiterook are defended by their own fierce fighters and a magically-enhanced vine and birch wall. The settlement lies on the banks of the Thundering River.
2028651570:
Mourning Burrows

The Mourning Burrows were once a mass burial ground for Kellids who lived in Druma long ago. Today, Razmiran insurgents who survived their failed raid on Twingate have settled in the interconnected tunnels between the many mounds here.
2049450680:
Claw Point

Claw Point is the northernmost point in Brevoy. It is located on the shores of the Lake of Mists and Veils and is just north of the beginning of the Icerime Peaks. While generally uninhabited, this escarpment of wind-eroded rock features a miserable stone bunker where a handful of mercenaries from House Lodovka are stationed each year. The outpost houses a significant magical item for communication—an emerald crystal ball—which allows scouts to alert the mages of Limstatic Academe in Winterbreak of any external threats. Thanks to this warning system, countless attempts to overtake Claw Point by Iobarian warlords have ended in mutual destruction at best. Alongside help from the Limstatic Academe, attacks are often halted by strangely unaccountable windstorms, avalanches, and hail.
2058723950:
Wisher's Well

Wisher's Well is one of the lesser known Thassilonian ruins in the greater Sandpoint area. This circular stone tower is 30 feet high, and the interior drops away into a 100-foot-deep shaft, ending in an underwater cavern connected to Sog's Bay.

This submerged complex was formerly the laboratory of Mazmiranna, who later abandoned it when she became the fifth runelord of greed. It has since been taken over by the albino alghollthu master Vorimorath, who has been drawn to Wisher's Well by the promise of Thassilonian treasures and the alghollthu master has restored Mazmiranna's wards and guardians. Myriad monsters also dwell in the complex, including Vorimorath's ulat-kini and faceless stalker minions, the latter of which have been modified to be able to breathe water.
2068273890:
Three Stars

The Three Stars are a set of three pyramids in southeastern Katapesh, within 20 miles of the Katapeshi coastline and Obari Ocean. The pyramids are made of yellow stone and topped with a large shining crystal that supposedly repairs itself if damaged.

Each pyramid is only 12 feet tall, small when compared with Katapeshi sites like the Red Pyramid or the Glass Pyramids and tiny when compared with Osirion's vast structures. There are no known entrances to the pyramids, and they are subject to the usual rumors suggesting that the pyramids hide either a dragon's horde, a dead king's treasure, or undead genies.

One of the more unusual and most prominent rumors is that the pyramids hide the remains of some sort of benevolent being described as either beings of living flame, beautiful maidens, or feathered serpents (possibly describing a muse azata) who sleeps beneath the pyramids.

The rumours also suggest that one day the sleepers will awaken. One such story claims that this will happen when Golarion becomes too chaotic, while according to another rumor there is a fourth pyramid that can be used to awaken the sleepers.
2083452770:
Egede

The port city of Egede (pronounced eh-GEHD-eh) is located on the Lake of Mists and Veils. It is the second-largest city in the Mendev, and as the end of the River Road, is the first Mendevian city that most crusaders entered during the period of the Mendevian Crusades.

From Egede, trade from neighbouring nations (in particular Brevoy, since the recent fall of House Rogarvia) usually flows into Mendev, as did crusaders traveling from the south through the River Kingdoms and Numeria. During the Mendevian Crusades, exhausted soldiers were often sent to Egede for much needed rest and relaxation, whether that was for prayer at the temples or a trip to the covert brothels and bawdy houses that thrived there. A great deal of work was also done here in support of the war effort, often by travelers thoroughly bankrupted by unscrupulous merchants on their way to the crusades.

The growth of industry, trade, and business proved helpful to the crusaders' cause, but some have begun to worry that the city is becoming a den of sin and debauchery.
2088625620:
Hallein Town

Hallein Town is a small Lastwall settlement located on the Path River downstream of Vigil. Situated at the foot of a high tarn rising from the shores of Lake Hallensee, access to it by land is almost impossible. Hallein Town is the home of the workers for the salt mines in the Virlych hills of nearby Ustalav, and provides vital trade goods for the rest of the nation.
2108523030:
Viperwall

Viperwall is a notorious keep built on the eastern shores of Ember Lake in central Varisia. Its many conical towers and serpent carvings emit a sickening green light and poisonous fumes, so it is to no surprise that the place is greatly avoided by the local population.

Rumors suggest that the castle was built in the mid-fifth millennium AR by serpentfolk who traveled up from Sekamina through the Mobhad Leigh in order to create a surface outpost for their people. Others believe that the fortress has existed since the time of Thassilon. Deep within the keep is a portal to the ruins of Sverenagati.
2115709560:
Pagoda of the Mantis

2 locations:
  • Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters
  • Hospice of Serenity
2122174990:
Crimsonlight Dig

The Crimsonlight Dig is one of the few archaeological sites in Kahlehlin. So far, the dig has unearthed ruins, an obsidian menhir, several glowing red stones, and more.
2132442080:
Nightmoss Camp

As of 4710 AR, a plant called nightmoss that grows in towering cypress trees throughout the Embeth Forest was in high demand. A small number of Uringen residents use their secret formulae to brew the moss into powerful potions that strengthen the body and heal physical conditions. A human druid named Erinin Thulgath stole the secret of nightmoss's healing properties and fled to establish Nightmoss Camp at the southeastern edge of the forest, where Thulgath and her group brew potent alchemical products.
2136529780:
Trollheim

The city of Trollheim can be found in Hagreach, the easternmost of the Linnorm Kingdoms, hard against its border with Irrisen and on the banks of the Rimeflow River. Trollheim has no current king and is ruled by Castellan Freyr Darkwine.
